tests: the straight-edge test (lines stay crisp and true at three metres)

pattern_tip.text: Stand three metres back from the garment and apply the straight-edge test. Look for absolute clarity in the boundaries. If the edges of a {recommended_pattern_1} begin to bleed, blur, or vibrate into the background colour at this distance, leave it there. You require clean impact to anchor your styling. A blurred print disrupts your self-contained visual logic. Check the linework specifically. The shapes must hold their defined borders perfectly. A warm motif needs this strict geometry to succeed without looking messy. Trust your immediate read. If you have to squint to decipher the shape across a shop floor, the pattern lacks the polished execution you require.
pattern_tip.sectionIntro: Use this visual check to ensure any print serves your architecture rather than fighting it.
tests: the straight-edge test (lines stay crisp and true at three metres)

venus_aries__moon_leo__fire_dominant.coreFormula: clean impact + athletic space + one hot accent
venus_aries__moon_leo__fire_dominant.closing: Trust your physical instincts completely, letting your hands and body verify your personal standard: clean impact + athletic space + one hot accent.

style_core.text:
Pick it up and judge every new piece by velocity. Put the garment on and test your working radius. Swing your limbs wide, drop into a deep lunge, and abruptly twist your torso. If the fabric hesitates, catches, or drags, reject it immediately. You demand clothing engineered for sudden motion rather than static posing. Picture a sleek navy technical trench coat worn over sharply tailored trousers, completed by a single blindingly warm gold collar pin. That exact combination perfectly demonstrates clean impact + athletic space + one hot accent.

Build your collection with slow, ruthless patience. Wait months for the exact high-gloss leather jacket or precisely tailored wool trousers that actively permit a full sprint. Once those exceptional items hang in your wardrobe, deploy them at lightning speed. Drift through your options by immediate instinct each morning. Your highly polished appearance relies on strict curation during the buying phase, leaving you completely free to move swiftly and decisively through the rest of your life.
style_core.sectionIntro: This is your core operational blueprint, the strictly enforced standard for every item you own.
tests: the radius test

textures_good.text: Build your wardrobe around polished materials that hold their shape and deliver a warm, self-contained energy. Rely on {texture_good_1} for sharp shirting that holds a crease and snaps visually, and choose {texture_good_2} for knife-edge tailoring that never requires a steamer. These structural bases create the athletic space you need to showcase one hot accent. Amplify that final focal point with {texture_good_3} for statement footwear and accessories that reflect maximum light. Close to the skin, use {texture_good_4} for slip dresses and linings that gleam under low light. Before purchasing, execute the snap test. Pull the hem tight between your thumbs and release it. The fabric must spring back to attention instantly to earn a place on this list.

textures_bad.text: Reject anything that blurs your edges or clings without resistance. Apply a crush check to every new garment. Squeeze the material tightly in your fist for five seconds and release it. If it stays wrinkled or slumps lifelessly against your palm, drop it. You need textiles that dictate their own volume to deliver clean impact. Avoid {texture_bad_1} entirely. Its fuzzy surface absorbs light and muddies your warm colour palette. Step away from {texture_bad_2}. It hangs weakly and stretches out of proportion by midday. Pass over {texture_bad_3}. The flat finish drains the physical intensity you require. Let your fingers decide. If the material feels flimsy or overly porous, it fails completely.
textures_bad.sectionIntro: Eliminate materials that dilute your physical boundaries.
tests: the bounce test

textures_sweet_spot.text: Contrast {sweet_spot_keyword_1} with {sweet_spot_keyword_2} to guarantee your clean impact. Your chosen textiles must command their own volume. Execute the crease test on any prospective trouser. Fold a length of {texture_good_1} in half and pinch down hard on the edge. Let go and watch it smooth out. If the fold line lingers, leave the item behind. You need this rapid recovery to maintain architectural tension around your frame. Anchor a stiff, matt weave against a slick surface. Picture a heavy cotton sleeve pushed up to reveal warm gold hardware. Rely on physical resistance. Your clothing must feel crisp, heavily structured, and ready to slice through the air.
textures_sweet_spot.sectionIntro: Nail your ideal combination of cloth to control your structure.
tests: the bounce test

palette_narrative.text: Your colours run boldly warm. Build your foundation using {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_2} as heavy, grounding base layers. Substitute stark white with {core_colour_3} to bounce light up to your face. Let {core_colour_4} serve as your temperature bridge. Reach for a relief tone only when a combination feels visually relentless and your eyes crave a temporary resting point. To test a garment, squint at it in the fitting room mirror. You must see clean impact and clear athletic space, interrupted by exactly one hot accent using {accent_colour_1} or {accent_colour_2}. Pass over muddy pastel, icy grey, and dusty sage. Avoid the trap of a restrictive dark monochrome look that stifles your presence. Fix this by breaking the silhouette with a massive gleaming gold cuff or a bright poppy red shoe.
palette_narrative.sectionIntro: Treat your colour choices as a definitive boundary for building volume, never a paintbox for blending in.

traps.failure: a restrictive dark monochrome look that stifles your presence
traps.fix: break the silhouette with a massive polished gold cuff or a bright poppy red shoe

occasions_work.text: At work, your core formula dictates your foundation: clean impact + athletic space + one hot accent. Construct your strict silhouette using sharp wool twill and crisp cotton poplin to set a rigid outer shell. Keep the garments loose enough to permit unhindered movement. You must remain self-contained. Finish the arrangement with a warm, polished metal watch to break the uniformity. A mundane commute or a long morning in the boardroom does not require surrendering your bold expression. Avoid soft knits that compromise your edges. Choose heavy silk satin blouses or stiff patent leather shoes to maintain your boundary. Apply the swivel chair test. Spin quickly in your office seat. If the blazer restricts your shoulders, take it off. Your workplace attire must remain uncompromising.
occasions_work.sectionIntro: Deploy your strict personal baseline in a professional environment without diluting your edge.
tests: a school run or a corner shop dash does not require surrendering your softness (Cove)

occasions_intimate.text: In close, low-light settings, apply your exact equation: clean impact + athletic space + one hot accent. Keep your layers stark. A heavy silk satin slip dress in deep bronze provides the foundation and the breathing room. Add thick gold cuffs to bring warm metal into the frame as the final focal point. Do not dilute your edges for intimacy. Even a late dinner requires architectural precision. Layer crisp cotton poplin under a matt camel coat. Select a saturated ruby ring to catch the candlelight. Conduct the drape check before you buy. Hold the garment by the shoulders and drop it onto a chair. It must hold a recognisable shape rather than collapsing into a puddle. Flimsy fabrics fail when the lights drop.
occasions_intimate.sectionIntro: Master close-quarters dressing by controlling exactly how your garments behave in low light.
tests: a school run or a corner shop dash does not require surrendering your softness (Cove)

occasions_daily.text: A Tuesday morning grocery run does not mean surrendering your high-gloss armour. Do not reach for a worn grey sweatshirt. Anchor your daily errands in an oversized leather bomber jacket layered over rigid cotton trousers. Step into a rich oxblood patent leather boot. Maintain a strict, self-contained boundary between you and the pavement. Your standard always requires clean impact + athletic space + one hot accent. Keep your metal hardware warm and highly reflective. Look for a shining gold bag clasp or a bright brass belt buckle. Pin a vivid fire opal to your lapel before leaving the house. Test your mobility on the pavement. Step up onto a high kerb. If the trousers catch on your knee, leave them at home.
occasions_daily.sectionIntro: Apply your standard rigorously, refusing to dilute your polished finish for mundane tasks.
tests: a school run or a corner shop dash does not require surrendering your softness (Cove)

You need substantial zips and buckles that hold their own inside an athletic space. Steer clear of {excluded_finish} surfaces. Beaten, aged, or tarnished textures appear exhausted and ruin your sharp geometry. Anchor your choices in highly reflective warm metals that flash visibly from a block away. Pick up the clasp or chain before making a decision. A gleaming photograph deceives the eye. Execute the gravity check. Drop the piece into your cupped hand. If a {metal_1} ring feels light, hollow, or fails to strike your palm with a distinct thud, leave it behind.
hardware_metals.sectionIntro: Treat your hardware as structural equipment, choosing metals that enforce your strict geometry.

Apply the shadow test before you commit. Cup your hand over the stone to block the overhead store lighting. The gem must still throw a blinding reflection even in the dimness. Hollow settings or dull cuts will not anchor your look. Avoid clouded or raw stones entirely. An opaque finish drains the energy from your presentation and looks distinctly tired. You need strictly polished, warm settings. Keep the overall construction slick and self-contained to maintain a flawless boundary.
hardware_stones.sectionIntro: Anchor your presence by testing every stone for pure density and high-gloss refraction.

Those subdued finishes look dull and compromise the sharp boundaries of your presentation. Rely strictly on an ultra-reflective warm sheen that projects total authority. A pristine gloss acts as a rigid mirror and delivers the clean, definitive geometry you require.

Set the item on a display table and walk twenty paces down the hall. Turn back and look at the bold silhouette. When evaluating a shoe, set it on the floor directly under a single bright ceiling bulb. If the toe box does not bounce a sharp, concentrated beam straight back at your eyes, leave it in the shop. You require a mirror finish that stands completely alone. A polished, warm gleam grounds the foot in total self-containment. Check the soles for thick, unyielding leather. Apply the bend test to the toe. Flex it upward with your thumbs. If the surface wrinkles into a matt crease rather than maintaining a stark curve, the hide is too weak.

Apply the squint drop test in the fitting room. Throw the patterned shirt onto a chair and narrow your eyes. The graphic separation must still strike the eye like a sharp architectural blueprint. If the motif collapses into a murky blur, drop the piece. Pass over {avoid_pattern_1} and {avoid_pattern_2} entirely. They introduce unnecessary visual clutter into your athletic space. A bold motif printed on a heavy canvas skirt must read instantly as deliberate geometry. It must never dissolve into a vague texture. Test the scale of your pieces rigorously. If you deploy a warm silk pocket square as your one hot accent, the design must remain distinct even when folded. Do not settle for visual confusion. Trace the printed borders with your index finger. If the edges feel visually ambiguous, the pattern fails.

Before you pay, apply the mirror walk. Hold the bag against your coat and stride ten paces backward away from the glass. If the geometry of the bag blurs into your outerwear from that distance, swap it out. Your final choice must deliver clean impact.

venus_aries__moon_sagittarius__fire_domi.coreFormula: clean impact + fast movement + one hot accent
venus_aries__moon_sagittarius__fire_domi.closing: Your style guide is a map of your own instincts, built on clean impact + fast movement + one hot accent.

style_core.text:
Your physical compass relies on speed. When you put a garment on, gauge how quickly it lets you walk, turn, and leave. If the cloth catches on your knee, drags at your shoulder, or makes you hesitate at the door, take it off. You need momentum. Trust the entrance test. Stand fully dressed before you step outside. If your outfit makes you pause to tweak a lapel or tug a hem, it fails. You want clothes that clear the way for immediate action. Keep your temperature warm and your finishes gleaming.

Your foundational equation is clean impact + fast movement + one hot accent. Picture a streamlined, structured jacket over a fine knit, worn with trousers that fall sharply over an aerodynamic boot, finished with a single piece of high-shine geometric jewellery. That is your template. Build your wardrobe slowly, but assemble your outfits rapidly. Curate individual pieces with strict precision so that when you pull them together, they fuse instantly. You operate best as a self-contained unit. Your clothes must let you strike quickly and step forward without looking back.
style_core.sectionIntro: This is your foundational logic, the core rulebook for how your clothes must behave on your body.
tests: the entrance test

Deploy {texture_good_4} for slip dresses and linings that gleam under low light to carry that one hot accent. Before buying, apply the deep fold check. Pinch the fabric and observe the interior shadow. If the crevice looks dead or swallows the room's illumination, leave the garment behind. You need a polished surface that bounces light back sharply. Let gravity and friction guide your choices. Build your core from the following.

You require textiles that slice through the glare and throw a hard, precise outline. This sharp boundary ensures fast movement across your silhouette. Warm, polished finishes reflect the environment without letting anyone inside. Keep the friction high and entirely self-contained. Finish the combination with one hot accent in a high-gloss finish to snap the focus.

palette_narrative.text: Your temperature is strictly warm. Build your foundation with {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_2}, relying on {core_colour_3} near the face instead of stark white. When you must drop the intensity, pull out your relief tone to lower the contrast. Avoid Muddy pastel, Icy grey, and Dusty sage. Check a shade by pressing the cloth against your inner forearm. If your veins look sunken or bruised, drop the garment. Beware the costume trap. Stacking glossy skins, heavy metal hardware, painted lips, and shadowed eyes drags high expression into theatre. Fix this error immediately. Isolate a single bright or metallic flash, then reduce every other element to dead, matt black.

A relentless schedule does not demand stiff armour. It requires polished cottons and yielding wools that let you sprint. Rely on your hands to evaluate these garments. Run your palm down the sleeve. If the fabric catches your skin, discard it. Sight deceives you, but friction remains true. Complete the structure with one hot accent. Fix a deep bronze clip to a lapel or step into warm patent leather flats. Your clothes must work at your precise speed.
occasions_work.sectionIntro: Apply these strict directives to your working hours.
tests: a packed day does not require hard clothes; it requires soft ones that can sprint (Wren)

occasions_intimate.text: In dim settings, clean impact leads your strategy. Reach for a golden camel shirt in heavy silk satin, and notice how the material catches a warm glow. Apply this rule to close-quarters dressing. Long dinners call for fluid garments that maintain a strict boundary without suffocating the wearer. Anchor the foundation with a sharp wool twill trouser. Fasten heavy, deep bronze jewellery near the face to direct attention. Assess the anchor weight before putting it on. Drop the necklace into your cupped hand. If it feels hollow or light, leave it in the box. Substantial materials hang properly when you sit, while flimsy synthetics buckle. Avoid lightweight items for evening events. Let the pure mass of the objects guide your final selections.
occasions_intimate.sectionIntro: Scale your proportions and textures for close proximity and low light.

occasions_daily.text: When you run to the chemist for paracetamol, hold your ground. Do not downgrade to sluggish jersey. Errands require smooth, yielding pieces that easily navigate the tarmac without sacrificing shape. Maintain your standard and demand clean impact + fast movement + one hot accent. Anchor a golden camel silk satin blouse with crisp cotton poplin. Add a highly polished bronze cuff. Test the outfit with a staircase sprint. Take two steps at a time. Mirrors deceive, but physical restriction never lies. If a seam bites into your thigh or drags across your shoulders, discard the item. Pick pieces that slice through the air while retaining a warm, lacquered exterior.
occasions_daily.sectionIntro: Approach a standard Tuesday exactly as you would a major presentation, using identical rules of engagement.
tests: a packed day does not require hard clothes; it requires soft ones that can sprint (Wren)

hardware_metals.text: Your metal strategy requires dominant warm tones to secure the outfit. Choose {metal_1} or {metal_2} with a fiercely polished surface to guarantee pure clean impact. Scratched or distressed hardware looks exhausted and absorbs light when you need it to flash. Keep your warm metals utterly pristine so they reflect the sun instantly from a distance. Restrict any secondary {metal_3} to tiny functional rivets. It must never compete with the primary metal. When evaluating a thick cuff, perform the table test. Set the item down on a hard wooden surface. If it clatters thinly rather than landing with a dull thud, it lacks the required density. A high-shine {metal_1} collar serves perfectly as your one hot accent. Keep every piece devoid of {excluded_finish} treatments. Absolute slickness maintains fast movement from head to toe.
hardware_metals.sectionIntro: Treat hardware as the crucial final strike that seals the silhouette.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: oxidised, hammered, or antiqued finishes look tired and lack crispness

hardware_stones.text: Demand high density and severe clarity when selecting {stone_1} and {stone_2}. Set them in undeniably warm metal. Avoid hammered, oxidised, or antiqued finishes. These treatments lack absolute crispness. Test the mineral integrity of a gem by pressing it against your cheek. If it warms up immediately like plastic, reject it. Genuine stones pull heat away from the skin. Stay strictly in highly polished settings that throw brilliant reflections across a room. This hardware functions as one hot accent, anchoring your self-contained aesthetic without relying on extraneous detail. The resulting clean impact supports your requirement for fast movement.

Visuals mislead, but structural fluidity never lies. Reject any item treated with dulling chemical oxidation. These artificially aged textures read as tired and ruin your clean impact. Instead, demand a mirror-like polish in a strictly warm metal tone that pierces through a crowd. A heavy, solid cast piece provides that essential one hot accent. Let the physical gravity of the object dictate your final purchase.

Enforce this ratio by wearing strictly one or two strong pieces per look. Start with statement jewellery, selecting a high-polish yellow gold cuff. The metal must maintain an immaculate polished surface to cast a sharp, warm reflection. Subject every addition to the gravity check. Let your arm hang loose at your side. The metal must pull down firmly, registering as a deliberate anchor through solid architecture alone.

Place the boot on the floor in an unlit room and shine a single harsh beam from your phone over the toe. If the shape does not immediately flash back a sharp structural outline, put the item down. Mirror finishes catch the slightest gleam to define your stance. Use a deep bronze patent brogue or a heavy ankle boot to deliver one hot accent at ground level. Check every buckle, zip, and eyelet. The hardware must feature rich yellow gold to lock in your warm palette and ensure clean impact.

If your silhouette loses its sharp edge or looks visually cluttered, remove the piece causing the distraction. Every item must survive as a stark, self-contained block. Look for a bag base that stands perfectly upright when set on a flat surface. If the structure sags or slouches, leave it behind. Clean impact demands absolute rigidity.

Apply the twenty-pace test to everything you consider. Walk to the opposite side of the shop and look back at the rail. If the design dissolves into a blur, walk out. The motif must stay deliberate and legible from afar. Tailored garments draw their geometry from structural weaves like {recommended_pattern_1} and {recommended_pattern_2}. Fluid pieces require the stark boundaries of {recommended_pattern_3} to maintain shape. Choose warm, polished contrasts that define the edges of your silhouette. Drive fast movement through your styling by pairing these sharp lines with one hot accent. Pass over anything tentative. Reject {avoid_pattern_1} and {avoid_pattern_2} outright. They lack the necessary tension.

You require sharp boundaries and high contrast to achieve clean impact. A pattern must communicate deliberate intent regardless of the environment. If the print resembles a fading ink stain, it fails the test. Absolute clarity is non-negotiable for a polished finish. Ground a highly legible motif with one hot accent in a bold, warm accessory. Check your reflection in the darkest corner of the space. The printed lines must remain entirely distinct.

venus_aries__moon_libra__air_dominant.coreFormula: clean impact + cool composure + one hot accent
venus_aries__moon_libra__air_dominant.closing: Your style guide is a map of your own instincts, built on clean impact + cool composure + one hot accent.

style_core.text:
Trust how a garment behaves in physical space. Pick up a tailored jacket or a structured shirt and hold it by the shoulders. Let the fabric drop. Watch the hemline. If the edge collapses inward or loses its crisp shape, leave it on the rail. Call this the gravity drop test. You are checking for structural memory and clear separation from the body. The piece must hold its own distinct perimeter in the air. Gather these structural pieces methodically over time, but combine them rapidly each morning.

Your wardrobe relies entirely on this specific formula: clean impact + cool composure + one hot accent. Picture a razor-sharp silk trench coat layered over a simple cashmere crewneck sweater, finished with a single, intensely vivid leather clutch. The base garments provide a warm, structured foundation. The solitary vivid addition provides a sudden strike of energy. Rely on sharp outlines and physical separation to do the heavy lifting. Trust your eyes to trace the distinct edges of the collar and the precise cut of the cuff.
style_core.sectionIntro: This is your fundamental operating system, the physical principles and core equation that will dictate every purchase.
tests: the fall test

textures_good.text: Your foundation demands clean impact and cool composure. Achieve this by selecting warm textiles that maintain strict physical boundaries without stiffness. Choose {texture_good_1} for shirting with clean architectural boundaries and pure separation. Use {texture_good_2} for close-to-body base layers with sharp lines and easy movement. Ground the lower half in {texture_good_3} for trousers that drape cleanly and resist creasing through a long day. Finish with {texture_good_4} for lightweight mobile outerwear that blocks wind and weighs little. Apply the pinch test before you buy. Squeeze the hem tightly between your thumb and index finger. If the fold collapses into mush, leave the garment on the rack. You need materials that hold their shape to successfully anchor one hot accent. Proceed with the ranked list below.
textures_good.sectionIntro: Build your physical foundation on materials that pass strict tactile tests and support your core formula.

rankedItems.useCase: shirting with clean architectural boundaries and pure separation

rankedItems.useCase: close-to-body base layers with sharp lines and easy movement

rankedItems.useCase: trousers that drape cleanly and resist creasing through a long day

rankedItems.useCase: a frictionless fluid layer added without bulk

rankedItems.useCase: breathable separation over fluid drapes in warmer weather

textures_bad.text: Run the inside of your wrist against {texture_bad_1}. If the friction catches or pulls, drop the item. It will buckle against your frame. You require absolute precision to sustain your clean impact, and anything flimsy destroys that sharpness. Brush your knuckles over {texture_bad_2}. If the fibres snag on dry skin or project an oily glare, leave it. Your warm palette requires deep, matt textures to absorb light properly. Slick materials scatter light irregularly. {texture_bad_3} poses a similar threat. Dig your fingertips into the weave. If the threads separate easily and fail to snap back, leave it on the rack.
textures_bad.sectionIntro: Identify the materials that sabotage your form before you even try them on.
tests: the edge test

textures_sweet_spot.text: Your sweet spot pairs {sweet_spot_keyword_1} with {sweet_spot_keyword_2}. Execute the knuckle crush test. Wring the sleeve of any item tightly in your fist. Open your hand. The cloth must spring flat immediately. If it retains a crushed wrinkle, discard it. A polished {texture_good_1} guarantees cool composure. Maintain a warm tone across your layers to ground the silhouette. Mix dual register metals to introduce controlled contrast. Add one hot accent to complete the logic.
textures_sweet_spot.sectionIntro: Nail your fabric pairings to ensure your items perform exactly as intended.
tests: the edge test

palette_narrative.text: Your palette operates on a strictly warm baseline. Anchor your foundation with {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_2}, using {core_colour_3} as a stark light alternative and {core_colour_4} to bridge opposing tones. Test any new piece by pressing it against your bare forearm in midday sun. If the dye strips the natural warmth from your skin, abandon the garment. When visual contrast feels severe, rely on your relief tone to soften the geometry. Leave icy pastel, neon brights, and cold blue-grey on the rack. Beware the frost trap. Unbroken layers of grey register as an absence of intent. Correct this instantly. Snap a mirror-finish cuff onto your wrist, step into a patent leather boot, or thread one hot accent through the silhouette using {accent_colour_1} or {accent_colour_2}. This exact calibration guarantees clean impact while maintaining cool composure.
palette_narrative.sectionIntro: Master your precise colour boundaries by testing every shade against your skin.

traps.failure: total ice: unbroken grey on grey can read as absence rather than composure
traps.fix: add the polished signal, a mirror-finish cuff, a patent boot, or one line of electric colour

occasions_work.text: At the desk, cool composure takes the lead. Establish this state using worsted wool trousers paired with crisp cotton poplin shirting. Keep the palette anchored in warm taupe or camel. These matt fabrics ensure your silhouette delivers clean impact at a glance. Perform the chair swivel test. Sit at your desk and turn sharply. Your garments must shift cleanly without binding. A turbulent schedule gives you no excuse to surrender your proportions. Leave oversized knits on the hanger. Maintain strict lines with fine ribbed merino that skims the ribcage without restricting your arms. Once the shape is set, introduce one hot accent. A heavy gold and silver chain or a bright silk scarf provides exactly enough heat to activate the outfit.
occasions_work.sectionIntro: Construct your workplace uniform by applying your core style formula.
tests: a market morning does not require abandoning proportion (Breeze)

occasions_intimate.text: A dim room softens sharp outlines. Compensate by trusting the physical reality of the cloth. Select a fine ribbed merino sweater in warm taupe or camel and run your hands down the torso. The dense knit must fall straight to the hem without clinging. A late supper does not require abandoning proportion. Lift your arms. If the shoulder seam slides down your bicep, put the piece back. Mix your dual register metals. Pair a heavy silver ring with fine gold jewellery to reflect candlelight. If you require a sharper silhouette, a crisp cotton poplin shirt provides instant structure. Ground the frame in worsted wool trousers to ensure your clean impact survives the evening.
occasions_intimate.sectionIntro: Navigate low-light settings by relying on shape and cloth over surface details.
tests: a market morning does not require abandoning proportion (Breeze)

occasions_daily.text: Apply the tension test. Grip the fabric behind the collar of your camel gabardine jacket and pull gently. If the seam yields or feels weak, leave the item on the hanger. A quick run to the chemist provides no excuse to drop your structural standard. Buying fresh produce requires the exact same baseline as a scheduled meeting. Build your foundation with matt dark denim. Apply your guiding equation every single time you get dressed. Your standard remains clean impact + cool composure + one hot accent. Evaluate the construction of your casual wear rigorously. Heavy seams and rigid drape tell the truth. Fasten a polished silver cuff over a warm mahogany silk sleeve to provide necessary heat.
occasions_daily.sectionIntro: Treat your morning routine as a deliberate and exacting drill.
tests: a market morning does not require abandoning proportion (Breeze)

hardware_metals.text: Your hardware strategy operates on two separate tracks. For jewellery sitting directly on the skin, select {personal_metal_1} or {personal_metal_2} to establish a warm baseline. Treat structural elements differently. Secure your zips, shoe buckles, and bag clasps strictly with {structural_metal_1} or {structural_metal_2}. Steer clear of artificially aged hardware. Oxidised copper, tarnished brass, and beaten surfaces create visual clutter against a modern silhouette. Maintain an immaculate polished finish by choosing bright silver, mirror-finished steel, or brilliant white gold, and exclude {excluded_finish} entirely. Evaluate a belt or bag by dropping the clasp into your palm. Focus entirely on the immediate, uncompromising weight. Rely on that sheer density to anchor the garment and deliver clean impact.
hardware_metals.sectionIntro: Master the physical metalwork that fastens and finishes your wardrobe.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: antiqued brass, hammered copper, and artificially aged metal read as clutter against a present-tense style
traps.fix: keep to polished silver, platinum, white gold, and mirror chrome

hardware_stones.text: Select your stones for high density and pristine clarity. Choose {stone_1} or {stone_2} to capture light effectively. Execute the light strike test. Hold the gem directly under a bright bulb. A high-density, pristine stone captures the beam cleanly to successfully anchor a warm palette. Ignore opaque or clouded inclusions. They diffuse the light and weaken the stone's presence. Restrict the surrounding mount to mirror chrome, platinum, white gold, or polished silver. This bright foundation delivers clean impact. Let the flawless finish secure your cool composure, allowing the pure stone to serve as your one hot accent.
hardware_stones.sectionIntro: Evaluate your stones and metal finishes by their physical mass and visual clarity.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: antiqued brass, hammered copper, and artificially aged metal read as clutter against a present-tense style
traps.fix: keep to polished silver, platinum, white gold, and mirror chrome

hardware_tip.text: Assess every piece of jewellery by its pendulum swing. Suspend a substantial, brilliantly mirrored {metal_1} link or a dense {stone_1} signet from your index finger. A proper piece pulls sharply downward, refusing to flutter. Depend on this tactile gravity to screen your choices. Discard any item with blackened grooves or simulated wear. Require an immaculate, perfectly polished exterior instead. A heavy piece resting against a warm sleeve effortlessly secures your one hot accent.
hardware_tip.sectionIntro: Evaluate your hardware using the primary test of mass.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: antiqued brass, hammered copper, and artificially aged metal read as clutter against a present-tense style
traps.fix: keep to polished silver, platinum, white gold, and mirror chrome

Restrict yourself to one or two strong pieces per look. Begin with silk scarves. Seek out a narrow silk band that provides a polished, soft glow against a wool collar. Enforce the closed-eye rule. Put the item on and close your eyes for three seconds. Open them. If a warm metallic watch dial commands your immediate attention, leave your neckline bare. If you knot that silk band at your throat, keep your wrists empty. Let physical weight rather than visual noise guide your final edit.

accessory_2.text: When selecting fine jewellery, look for a thin gold chain with a high polish finish. The reflective surface acts as one hot accent against a flat collar. This chain must deliver exactly one clear flash of warm light. If the design introduces multiple pendants or complicated textures, it fails the aesthetic brief. Keep the metal warm and the surface strictly glassy to maintain your cool composure. Execute the drag test. Pull the thin chain across the back of your hand. The highly polished surface must flow smoothly like heavy liquid without scratching your skin. Do not tolerate pieces that clatter like cheap industrial hardware.

A dual register mix of metals works perfectly here, provided the finish remains highly reflective. This bag frequently operates as your one hot accent against warm worsted wool. Before you step out, execute a final scan. Tap every reflective point on your body. If a high-shine clasp fights against heavy neck chains, you are broadcasting noise. Strip away the loudest item until the entire look transmits clean impact.

pattern_narrative.text: Ground your structured tailoring in precise geometries like {recommended_pattern_1}. Let your fluid garments take their rhythm from softer spacing through {recommended_pattern_2}. Your versatile approach requires an adaptive contrast rule. Keep tonal variations subtle to project cool composure in daily utility. When shifting gears, deploy a high-contrast {recommended_pattern_3} over a warm base to anchor your one hot accent. This ensures pure clean impact without overwhelming the eye. Verify every print with the straight-edge test. Step back three metres from the mirror. The boundaries of the motif must remain crisp and true. If the lines bleed into a muddy wash, drop the garment. Pass over {avoid_pattern_1}, {avoid_pattern_2}, and any scale that distorts when stretching across your shoulders. Rely on your spatial instincts to filter out visual static.

If the rigid geometry warps or the colours bleed across the boundary, leave the piece behind. You require strict architectural lines to anchor your cool composure. The print must remain sharply defined without losing its discipline. When you introduce one hot accent through a motif, the graphic must slice cleanly through the surrounding warm background. A pattern fails instantly if the boundary between colours loses its discipline.
pattern_tip.sectionIntro: Enforce strict visual boundaries on your prints to ensure they perform correctly.
tests: the straight-edge test (lines stay crisp and true at three metres)

venus_aries__moon_pisces__water_dominant.coreFormula: clean impact + blurred edges + one hot accent
venus_aries__moon_pisces__water_dominant.closing: Trust your hands and your own physical reactions to navigate this map of instincts, forever arriving at clean impact + blurred edges + one hot accent.

style_core.text:
Your physical compass relies on the lungs of a garment. Watch how the fabric takes in air and settles against your frame. A piece must glide without clinging and drape without suffocating. Apply the exhalation test to every new garment. Put the item on, take a deep breath in, and let it out completely. The material should settle around you in a warm, self-contained column of air, never restricting your ribs or catching statically on your skin. If the fabric feels stifling or refuses to move when you exhale, leave it in the shop.

Your guiding equation is clean impact + blurred edges + one hot accent. Picture a softly tailored topcoat left unfastened over a fine-gauge rollneck, finished with a single, highly polished sculptural ring. The outer silhouette remains structured, the transitions between layers dissolve into gentle shadows, and a solitary point of heat draws the eye. Construct this foundation slowly over seasons. Demand perfect movement, hold out for exact proportions, and deploy your chosen pieces rapidly on busy mornings. You know immediately when a shape works for your body. Trust that physical response.
style_core.sectionIntro: Memorise this core logic to build a wardrobe that works with your physical instincts rather than against them.
tests: the breath test

textures_good.text: Focus your hands on materials that deliver clean impact without rigid boundaries. Assess every new purchase by bunching the fabric in your fist. Squeeze tightly and release. If the material springs back into a warm, self-contained drape, the piece will serve you. If it holds a harsh crease, leave it on the rail. Seek {texture_good_1} for slips and blouses softened until the sheen turns to candlelight. Rely on {texture_good_2} for jumpers and cardigans that shelter without weight. Pick {texture_good_3} for dresses that trace the body with fluid, hidden depth. Build your outer layers with {texture_good_4} for unstructured protective coats that stay soft at every edge, keeping your blurred edges intact.
textures_good.sectionIntro: Train your hands to recognise the textiles that build your private sanctuary.

rankedItems.useCase: slips and blouses softened until the sheen turns to candlelight

rankedItems.useCase: jumpers and cardigans that shelter without weight

rankedItems.useCase: dresses that trace the body with fluid, hidden depth

textures_bad.text: Reject {texture_bad_1} the moment your fingers meet it. Trust your tactile nerves. They know when a fabric feels slick and artificial. Test {texture_bad_2} by rubbing two layers of the weave together rapidly. Listen for the friction. If the material emits a loud, plastic scrape instead of a quiet whisper, walk away. Your aesthetic requires natural airflow to maintain a warm, self-contained environment. Synthetic barriers suffocate the skin and dictate false shapes. A stiff {texture_bad_3} creates harsh boundaries where you need soft margins. Keep your foundation entirely natural and reserve any extreme synthetic finish exclusively for your one hot accent.
textures_bad.sectionIntro: Identify material failure in the hand before it ever reaches your skin.
tests: the breath test

textures_sweet_spot.text: Combine {sweet_spot_keyword_1} and {sweet_spot_keyword_2}. This exact pairing delivers clean impact. Drape a piece of {texture_good_1} across your bare forearm. You must feel an immediate, warm thermal regulation. If the material traps heat uncomfortably against your skin, abandon it. Rely on this physical sensation to guide you. A self-contained wardrobe requires materials that physically perform. Anchor these fabrics with heavy yellow gold or warm brass. Mirrors mislead. Your skin tells the truth.
textures_sweet_spot.sectionIntro: Master your materials by physically testing them before they ever reach your wardrobe.
tests: the breath test

palette_narrative.text: Keep your entire spectrum rooted in a strictly warm temperature. Construct your foundation with {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_2}. Frame these lighter tones using {core_colour_3} to define your geometry, and treat {core_colour_4} as your primary grounding element. When your vision feels overwhelmed by continuous richness, deploy your relief tone as a visual palate cleanser. For a deliberate focal point, introduce {accent_colour_1} or {accent_colour_2}. This precise application guarantees clean impact without unnecessary noise. Evaluate your outfits by squinting at them in a well-lit mirror. If the lighter tones bleed together, your geometry is missing and you must add a darker frame. Always pass over stark optic white, harsh neon, and pale pastel. Watch for the trap of structural dissolution. This occurs when you layer pale gauze over pale gauze with nothing underneath, causing your silhouette to vanish into blurred edges. Fix this instantly by restoring a single firm boundary. Resolve the outfit by adding a bias-cut slip that traces the body, integrating a fitted base layer, or placing {core_colour_3} directly against the skin.
palette_narrative.sectionIntro: Assign every shade in your wardrobe a strict structural job, relying on physical discipline rather than random decoration.

traps.failure: dissolution: pale gauze on pale gauze with nothing beneath and the silhouette disappears
traps.fix: restore one line: a bias-cut slip that traces the body, a fitted base layer, or the deep sea blue anchor worn closest to the skin

occasions_work.text: At work, clean impact takes the lead. Construct your work uniform around precise cuts in bitter chocolate or dark mahogany. This creates a sharp silhouette that signals self-contained focus. Support this clear outline with blurred edges. Let a heavy silk crepe shirt soften the lines around your neck and wrists. Add one hot accent, placing warm gold jewellery at the collarbone. Never compromise physical ease for formal presentation. Apply the pharmacy test before leaving the house. If standing in the queue for a prescription feels like physical suffering, take the pieces off. Your clothing must never distract you from the work itself.
occasions_work.sectionIntro: Master the professional environment by setting your own strict physical baseline.
tests: the pharmacy line: a trip to the pharmacy does not require suffering for the aesthetic (Slate)

occasions_intimate.text: In close, low-light settings, let blurred edges lead. Dim lighting flattens rigid shapes. Counter this by choosing washed silk or heavy silk crepe in dark mahogany. Bring in a single focal point in warm gold to capture the eye. Apply the late-night standing test before acquiring any evening piece. If you find yourself shivering or adjusting a restrictive seam under harsh overhead lights, the garment fails. True luxury does not require suffering. Layer matt lambswool over a bitter chocolate slip for drafty rooms. Pair matt fabrics with highly polished metals to spark your one hot accent.
occasions_intimate.sectionIntro: Evaluate your evening wear for dim lighting and close quarters.

occasions_daily.text: Running a morning errand does not require suffering in stiff fabrics. Keep your daily baseline comfortable and warm. Look for dense fibres. Reach for a dark mahogany washed silk shirt. Layer a matt boiled wool jumper over it. Add a polished brass ring. Your mundane routine always demands clean impact + blurred edges + one hot accent. Trust your physical comfort as your ultimate compass. Avoid flimsy synthetics entirely. Test your basics with the gravity check. Pick a sheer piece up by the shoulders and let it hang. If it floats aimlessly and lacks the dense substance to pull downwards, drop it back on the rail.

Introduce {metal_3} strictly as a focal point to execute your formula: clean impact + blurred edges + one hot accent. Mirror-polished metals create a harsh glare against soft, warm garments. Prevent this clash. Stick to matt and brushed finishes to keep the visual field cohesive. Apply the palm weight check. Weigh every new clasp or buckle in your bare hand. Visual presentation deceives the eye. Dense physical mass tells the truth.

A substantial stone drags heavily against the nap, while a hollow imitation skips over the surface. The sheer density of the object confirms its worth. You require stones that hold a warm tone and absorb light rather than refract it. Avoid highly polished bright silver. A mirror finish creates a sharp distraction against muted colours. Rely on matt and brushed textures to keep the entire look cohesive. Use a single piece of {stone_3} as your one hot accent. This ensures your aesthetic remains strictly self-contained.

If {metal_1} produces a thin, tinny rattle instead of a low, resonant thud, drop it. You need substantial weight to deliver clean impact against a warm {core_colour_1} knit. Avoid high-gloss chrome entirely. Mirror finishes cut too sharply against soft textures like boiled wool. Seek out flat, oxidised, or sandblasted surfaces instead. A softly antiqued {metal_1} setting for a dense {stone_1} keeps the visual boundaries gentle and the whole look cohesive.

Limit your hardware to one or two strong pieces per look. Begin with handbags. Look for full-grain leather. Insist on a completely matt body anchored by a single warm, polished clasp. Test every potential addition physically. Hold the bag by its strap and let it hang free to conduct the float test. A poorly constructed item feels flimsy and drags awkwardly. A worthy piece passes this check by keeping its precise shape while feeling distinctly buoyant and balanced against your hip.

Drop the fabric over your outstretched arm and watch how it descends. True silk drifts slowly through the air, settling softly without harsh creases. Trust this physical reaction over any label. A rigid drop indicates synthetic fibres. When selecting a scarf in deep bitter chocolate or dark mahogany, use it to introduce one hot accent into your ensemble. Narrow your eyes until the room goes soft. The motif must dissolve into a gradient of diffused warmth. If the graphic remains rigid and reads like a harsh barcode, put the item back on the rail. You require patterns that instantly melt into blurred edges from afar. Let your own vision dictate the choice rather than the sales tag. venus_aries__moon_scorpio__water_dominan.coreFormula: clean impact + quiet undercurrent + one hot accent
venus_aries__moon_scorpio__water_dominan.closing: Your style guide is a map of your own instincts, built on clean impact + quiet undercurrent + one hot accent.

style_core.text:
Assess garments by their breath and flow. When you try something on, take a deep inhalation and watch how the fabric responds to the expansion of your ribs. It must glide in tandem with your movement and resettle seamlessly. If the material clings rigidly to your skin or billows wildly away from your frame, leave it. Apply the shelter test directly using your own body: demand that your clothing acts as a precise, self-contained envelope that protects your space. Curate these foundation pieces with deliberate slowness over time, deploy them swiftly when getting dressed, and let your daily combinations drift by mood.

Construct your daily uniform around a strict equation of clean impact + quiet undercurrent + one hot accent to maintain your visual boundaries. Picture a structured, heavy wool coat with a completely matt finish layered over sharply tailored trousers. That is the foundation. Finish it with a solitary, highly polished ring in a warm tone resting on your index finger. This strategy anchors your presence firmly without inviting unwanted negotiation. The result is a highly deliberate aesthetic that shields you while signalling immense capability.
style_core.sectionIntro: Master the strict physical laws and precise boundaries that govern how you build your wardrobe.
tests: the shelter test

textures_good.text: To build a wardrobe with clean impact, you must select materials that feel entirely self-contained. Rely on {texture_good_1} for slips and blouses softened until the sheen turns to warm candlelight. Pick {texture_good_2} for jumpers and cardigans that shelter without weight. Source {texture_good_3} for dresses that trace the body with fluid, hidden depth. Anchor these lighter layers with {texture_good_4} for unstructured protective coats that stay soft at every edge. To verify a fabric belongs in your quiet undercurrent, apply the exhalation trap. Hold the textile against your inner wrist and blow out slowly. If the material traps the heat and feels instantly stifling, drop it. If it regulates your temperature and breathes with you, keep it. Rely on this specific sensory feedback to curate your rail.
textures_good.sectionIntro: Learn to evaluate a textile blindly to build your textural foundation.

It hangs stiffly and refuses to drape, undermining the fluid canvas needed to support your one hot accent. Trust your hands to detect these material failures before you ever try the piece on.

Drape it heavily over your bare shoulder and gauge how your neck muscles respond. If the piece allows you to drop tension completely, keep it. This bodily reaction establishes your quiet undercurrent. Pair this yielding base with a sharp shape to secure clean impact. Anchor the entire combination with one hot accent in a warm, matt finish. Avoid anything glossy or starched that forces you to hold your spine artificially straight. Trust your skin to detect the difference between true ease and synthetic construction.
textures_sweet_spot.sectionIntro: Master your material contrasts to anchor your daily choices.

palette_narrative.text: Establish a self-contained foundation by pairing {core_colour_1} with {core_colour_2}. Ground these lighter base layers using {core_colour_3} or {core_colour_4} to build sharp, structural definition. Every colour you adopt must carry a distinct, warm temperature. Deploy the relief tone sparingly as a soft note to break severe lines. Leave stark optic white, harsh neon, and icy pastel on the rack. Deliver clean impact by introducing {accent_colour_1} or {accent_colour_2} as a deliberate focal point. Avoid the trap of sinking into a shapeless, entirely dark silhouette. When you wear heavy layers, fracture that void with a glowing detail. Pick up a hammered gold cuff or slide a tortoiseshell comb into place. Let the physical weight of these distinct accents direct your daily dressing.

occasions_work.text: When dressing for work, let clean impact take the lead. Build the foundation with heavy silk crepe tailored in dark mahogany. Support this crisp exterior by slipping a quiet undercurrent close to the skin. Reach for washed silk or cloud-soft lambswool to ensure internal comfort. A busy commute or a midday shop dash does not mean you must sacrifice this deep, tactile softness. Trust your knuckles to select fibres that feel reassuring and warm. Rub the hem between your fingers behind your desk. If it scratches, leave it. Finish the look with one hot accent in polished gold to capture the office light.

Apply this strict rule for private evenings indoors. Moving from the dining table to the study does not require surrendering your yielding base layers for a rigid jacket. Maintain your clean impact. Finish the look with one hot accent in a warm metal. Let a thick brass collar or a single polished bronze cuff catch the candlelight.

Your constant baseline must be clean impact + quiet undercurrent + one hot accent. Run your thumb over the top seam of your trousers. venus_aries__moon_taurus__air_dominant.coreFormula: clean impact + sensory comfort + one hot accent
venus_aries__moon_taurus__air_dominant.closing: Your style guide is a map of your own instincts, built on clean impact + sensory comfort + one hot accent.

style_core.text:
Assess every garment by its architectural independence. Apply the flight test. Lift a tailored blazer or a pleated skirt off the hanger and drop it onto a flat surface. Watch how the fabric behaves. You require materials that retain their structure and separation as they land. If the item collapses into a shapeless puddle, leave it on the rack. Your clothes must float slightly away from your frame to keep the silhouette distinct. Demand polished weaves that hold a sharp edge. Build your collection slowly to secure these precise cuts.

Assemble your outfits around your strict formula of clean impact + sensory comfort + one hot accent. Picture a tailored gabardine trench coat layered over a fluid silk camisole and secured with a high-shine leather belt. The stiff exterior delivers structure. The soft inner layer provides physical ease against the skin. The belt supplies the focal point. Ensure your foundation pieces carry a warm undertone to anchor the polished finish. Put these components together swiftly each morning. Adapt the outer layers as your environment shifts throughout the day.
style_core.sectionIntro: Memorise this baseline logic to filter out mistakes before they reach your wardrobe.

textures_good.text: Your formula requires sensory comfort. Achieve this by selecting materials that hold their structure without fighting your joints. Sweep your hands over every piece. If a fabric cannot withstand an eight-hour train ride and emerge with a polished, warm finish, reject it entirely. Grab {texture_good_1} for shirting that delivers clean architectural boundaries and pure separation. Build your close-to-body base layers with {texture_good_2} to maintain sharp lines and continuous breathability. Select {texture_good_3} for trousers that drape precisely and resist creasing through a demanding schedule. Seal the system using {texture_good_4} for lightweight mobile outerwear that blocks wind and weighs nothing. These fibres guarantee clean impact.

Rough facets, raw crystals, and unpolished surfaces create visual static against a modern silhouette. Reject them entirely. accessory_2.text: Scarves act as the final link in your system of mobile layers. Source them exclusively in fine wool or pure silk. Insist on a completely matt finish. Avoid anything with a high sheen or a slippery grip. Apply the float test before you buy. Drop the textile from chest height. Watch the descent. If the material plummets in a heavy clump, reject the piece. If it catches the air and drifts down slowly, it passes. Cool, dark silk will absorb light and anchor the cool temperature of your clothing. Rely on this physical behaviour over printed patterns. Drag and air resistance reveal the true quality of a woven fibre.
accessory_2.sectionIntro: Subject your secondary accessories to strict physical trials to guarantee their quality.

rankedItems.name: Scarves
rankedItems.useCase: silk or fine wool, matt

tests: the float test

accessory_3.text: Anchor garments using medium full-grain leather belts. Run your thumb over the hardware to verify every buckle has a flat matt finish. Next, add a personal keepsake cast in aged silver or oxidised metal. The dark patina of these pieces grounds the look without breaking the cool temperature of the fabrics. When you finish adjusting your clothes, always apply the balance check. Ask yourself if the final piece of jewellery hovers seamlessly within the muted scheme. If the metal or leather visually sinks the outfit or drags the eye downward, take it off immediately. Surface appeal distracts, but actual heft confirms the quality. Trust somatic feedback to confirm the proportions are right.
accessory_3.sectionIntro: Anchor the silhouette with strict hardware rules and use physical weight to know when the outfit is complete.

pattern_narrative.text: Keep pattern contrast low and tonal. Tailored trousers and structured jackets take their cues from woven geometries like {recommended_pattern_1} and {recommended_pattern_2}. Fluid silk skirts take theirs from organic, descending lines. Pass over high-contrast graphics, {avoid_pattern_2}, and {avoid_pattern_1}. Subject every printed cloth to the underwater test. Pick it up and watch how the fabric distorts. The motif should resemble ambient light shifting across the seabed, never rigid street signage. Let structural behaviour dictate your choice. Cloth moves predictably, while printed illusions fail. Construct your shielding exterior from these subtle weaves. Throw on cool, matt mobile layers to complete the silhouette, ensuring the pattern remains a secondary detail.
pattern_narrative.sectionIntro: Evaluate print as a structural element, not a decorative afterthought.
tests: the underwater test (read like light moving on the seabed, not signage)

pattern_tip.text: Step back ten paces from the mirror and blur your vision. The ideal {recommended_pattern_1} should read like water rippling over stones, never like rigid geometry demanding attention. When selecting fabrics for mobile layers, look for motifs that diffuse into a muted, continuous wash of cool tones. Avoid anything that creates stark, distinct shapes from a distance. A successful print dissolves into the background texture, offering a subtle visual rhythm rather than a sharp focal point. Discard any piece that breaks this fluid illusion.
pattern_tip.sectionIntro: Apply the underwater test to assess the legibility of any printed garment.
tests: the underwater test (read like light moving on the seabed, not signage)

venus_cancer__moon_cancer__air_dominant.coreFormula: soft shelter + hidden depth + one sentimental keepsake
venus_cancer__moon_cancer__air_dominant.closing: Trust your physical instincts to navigate every garment you encounter, anchoring your decisions strictly in soft shelter + hidden depth + one sentimental keepsake.

style_core.text:
Look for clean separation in every garment. Evaluate how a piece holds its architectural line. Test the tension by running your thumb along the hem. If the edge collapses or the structure slumps, put it back. Construct this wardrobe deliberately over seasons, acquiring items piece by piece, and deploy them instantly. Let your internal barometer make the final call on any purchase. Step back and turn towards the door. If you feel no immediate knot of regret leaving the item on the hanger, keep walking.

Anchor everything you assemble to your core equation: soft shelter + hidden depth + one sentimental keepsake. Picture a sharply tailored coat with a cool, muted finish worn over a fine-gauge knit that sits perfectly flat at the collarbone. Secure the cuff over an inherited, unpolished ring. The outer layer defines your boundary. The crisp knit provides the interior structure. The ring grounds the entire composition.
style_core.sectionIntro: This is your structural blueprint, the exact physical criteria required to bring any item into your wardrobe.
tests: trust your gut and walk away

textures_good.text: Seek out {texture_good_1} for knitwear that compresses and bounces back. Build your soft shelter from {texture_good_3} in tailored coats that hold clean lines against the cool air. Rely on {texture_good_4} for fluid blouses that offer hidden depth beneath a heavy outer layer. Choose {texture_good_2} for bags and belts that mould to your grip and patinate over years of use. Test every new piece by pressing the material against your inner wrist. If the fabric scratches the sensitive skin or yields a squeaky friction, leave the garment on the rail.
textures_good.sectionIntro: Train your hands to recognise the materials that build your foundation.

textures_bad.text: Avoid {texture_bad_1}. Trap a fold of the cloth between your palms. If the material pushes back against your pressure, drop it immediately. Your foundation requires layers that yield, not stiff plastics that restrict physical movement. Reject {texture_bad_2} completely. Crush a sleeve in your fist. If it crinkles like a discarded wrapper or feels unnaturally slick, it fails the test. These harsh synthetics trap heat. They disrupt your cool, muted palette and will never sit in harmony beside your one sentimental keepsake. Train your hands to detect poor construction instantly.

tests: if a fabric resists your touch or feels squeaky between your fingers, leave it behind

textures_sweet_spot.text: Anchor your layers in a cool foundation of {sweet_spot_keyword_1} worn beneath {sweet_spot_keyword_2}. This combination builds an immediate soft shelter around your frame. Add a sweater in {texture_good_1} to provide weight. Assess the drape by gathering the hem and letting it drop. If the textile falls rigidly or catches awkwardly at the crease of your elbow, abandon it. Build a self-contained system using only fibres that absorb light and fall quietly.
textures_sweet_spot.sectionIntro: Combine your textiles to establish your exact personal baseline.
tests: if a fabric resists your touch or feels squeaky between your fingers, leave it behind

palette_narrative.text: Your temperature is decidedly cool. Build a self-contained foundation using {core_colour_1}, {core_colour_2}, and {core_colour_3} for your primary garments. Rely on {core_colour_4} as your strict white substitute. This muted base establishes your hidden depth. Reserve {accent_colour_1} and {accent_colour_2} for singular, deliberate accents in matt silk or brushed wool. Use your relief tone sparingly, applying it as a soft note to lift a heavy cashmere sleeve. Leave hot saturated red, stark optic white, and harsh neon on the rack. Avoid head-to-toe black. An unbroken dark column flattens the silhouette. Break it up with a {core_colour_4} knit layer, a {accent_colour_1} scarf, or a {core_colour_2} full-grain leather belt.
palette_narrative.sectionIntro: Control your palette by treating colour as a precise structural tool.

traps.failure: head-to-toe black reads like you gave up on the way out the door
traps.fix: break it up with a camel layer, a deep wine scarf, or a rich cognac belt

occasions_work.text: At work, let soft shelter lead your decisions. Judge a garment by its capacity to move. A brutal fourteen-hour shift requires pliable fabrics that sprint with your stride, not stiff armour that fights your body. Rely on fluid silk shirts and unstructured cashmere jackets. Let hidden depth support this base. Seek out matt finishes and heavy twills that only reveal their complex weave upon closer inspection. Anchor the entire composition with one sentimental keepsake. Fasten a cool silver locket or a vintage lapis pin to your collar. Trust the physical weight of that private detail to ground you, leaving the pliable wools to carry you through the day.
occasions_work.sectionIntro: Approach your daily work attire as a physical toolkit, demanding absolute ease and private meaning from every item.
tests: a packed day does not require hard clothes; it requires soft ones that can sprint (Wren)

occasions_intimate.text: In low light, prioritise touch over visual contrast. Your soft shelter leads here. A demanding evening does not require stiff construction. It requires pliant layers that endure until midnight. Choose cashmere knitwear in deep teal or brushed wool trousers in dove grey. Test the drape by rolling the cuffs. A heavy silk shirt in inky navy must pool fluidly at the wrists, never binding the forearms. Ground the silhouette with a single matt silver ring to supply hidden depth. Dim lighting catches cool, muted metal and softens the edges. Reject harsh details. Focus purely on how the cloth responds to your physical movement, keeping one sentimental keepsake as your sole hard anchor.
occasions_intimate.sectionIntro: Master the mechanics of low-light dressing for close dinners and private environments.
tests: a packed day does not require hard clothes; it requires soft ones that can sprint (Wren)

occasions_daily.text: A gruelling itinerary never requires restrictive armour. It demands pliant textiles that move at your pace without chafing. Test the stretch of a deep teal silk shirt or a dove grey jersey. Reach forward to grab a door handle and check for pulling across the back. A late-night run to the chemist for paracetamol does not suspend your baseline. Do not reach for discarded sweatpants. Pull on an inky navy knit and step into a pliable full-grain leather boot. Fasten a cool, matt white gold chain at your collar. Your standard remains constant in every mundane environment. The blueprint is always soft shelter + hidden depth + one sentimental keepsake. Evaluate garments by their quiet utility. Leave rigid items on the hanger.
occasions_daily.sectionIntro: Construct your everyday wardrobe for private endurance, not public exhibition.
tests: a packed day does not require hard clothes; it requires soft ones that can sprint (Wren)

hardware_metals.text: Base your hardware choices on a strictly cool foundation. Limit your selections to {metal_1}, {metal_2}, and {metal_3}. Avoid {excluded_finish} entirely. Highly polished, gleaming chrome hardware flashes abruptly against muted garments. Fix this distraction by insisting on brushed or matt textures that keep the visual plane cohesive. Always evaluate metal by its temperature. Press the buckle or clasp against your cheek. If the material feels warm and plastic, leave it on the shelf. Cool, weighty hardware secures the garment and supplies the hidden depth necessary to anchor a soft shelter.
hardware_metals.sectionIntro: Evaluate the hardware that fastens your garments and anchors your style.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

hardware_stones.text: Seek stones that pull light inward rather than refracting it back out. Your collection requires hidden depth, not surface glitter. Inspect {stone_1} and {stone_2} for dense inclusions or muted opacity. Tilt the setting beneath a lamp to judge its reflection. A matt, cloudy stone absorbs the beam and signals quality. Pay strict attention to the metal holding a {stone_3}. Highly reflective mounts will slice right through your muted fabrics, creating a distracting, sharp glare. Demand cool, brushed or strictly matt hardware to keep the piece cohesive.
hardware_stones.sectionIntro: Evaluate your stones by their density and how they absorb light.

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours

hardware_tip.text: Lift the {metal_1} piece or {stone_1} ring and weigh it in your palm. Appearance deceives, but gravity always tells the truth. If the item feels hollow, put it back. Your hardware must possess a heavy, quiet solidity to properly anchor your soft shelter. Watch out for highly reflective, polished surfaces. These glaring finishes cut harshly against your muted garments and ruin your visual cohesion. Correct this error by insisting on strictly matt or heavily brushed textures. These subdued finishes absorb light rather than reflecting it. They ensure your metals provide hidden depth instead of acting as a sharp distraction.
hardware_tip.sectionIntro: Evaluate your hardware by its physical mass.

accessory_1.text: Accessories anchor your daily equation. They sit against the soft shelter of your garments and ground the hidden depth of your layers. Treat a chosen piece of metal as the one sentimental keepsake that finishes the structure. Apply the rule of removal before crossing the threshold. Take off any item that clamours for attention. One strong piece is sufficient. Begin this process with handbags. Seek out dense full-grain leather or thick suede. Inspect the clasps and buckles. The metal must be cool and matt brushed. Assess the density by tapping the hardware. Flick a chain link or zip pull with your fingernail. If the metal rings with a hollow, tinny pitch, reject it outright. Heavy, silent hardware confirms craftsmanship.
accessory_1.sectionIntro: Accessories dictate the final weight of your silhouette, grounding the fabric with solid metal and thick leather.

Demand a strictly matt finish in deep teal or dove grey. Let the fabric drape naturally to reveal hidden depth within the textile. Scrutinise the overall composition in the mirror. Strip away any conflicting details. If an oversized wrap competes with a heavy silver collar, take the metal off. A solitary strong shape supplies sufficient gravity. Test the material by pulling it into a tight knot. Seek dense, muted fibres that hold the shape without buckling. You know the correct texture when you feel it resist gently.

Verify leather quality by bending the strap firmly back on itself. Trust a dense hide that curves smoothly without cracking the surface finish. Perform a strict final audit. Undo the clasp of any piece doing too much. One strong metal accent completes the uniform.

pattern_tip.text: Apply the Five Pace Fade. Hang any garment featuring {recommended_pattern_1} on a door and take five steps back. Squint your eyes. If the lines remain starkly legible, leave the piece on the rail. The right design must melt into a unified tone. This visual integration provides soft shelter from external noise. Seek out weaves combining {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_2} that rely on subtle shade shifts rather than rigid borders. Keep the scale fine. Close up, the material holds quiet complexity. From a distance, it becomes a single, cool sweep of heavy cloth. Trust your own eyes to judge the contrast. Sharp lines interrupt. Soft margins protect.

style_core.text:
Gauge the physical weight of a garment in your hands before you examine the cut. Your sensory compass relies on insulation. Look for dense textiles that absorb sound and light. Build your wardrobe with absolute slowness. Test every piece for a physical sensation of retreat. If a garment pushes your energy outward or demands immediate justification, drop it. You are constructing a self-contained system. Demand a cool, matt surface that keeps the external world at a calculated distance.

Your governing equation is soft shelter + hidden depth + one sentimental keepsake. Picture a heavy cocooning overcoat covering an intricately constructed inner layer, anchored by an inherited pocket charm kept completely out of sight. Keep the external presentation impenetrable. Keep the complexity entirely private. When evaluating a new acquisition, apply the withdrawal test. If your instinct hesitates or you feel even slightly exposed in the fitting room, trust your physical reluctance and walk away. Looks lie. Your physical instinct provides your ultimate compass.
style_core.sectionIntro: Master these foundational rules to establish the structural boundaries of your wardrobe.
tests: trust your gut and walk away

textures_good.text: Establish your wardrobe as a barrier against the outside world. To identify the correct materials, perform the friction check. Close your eyes and roll the fabric between your thumb and forefinger. Let insulation guide you. Visuals deceive, but physical resistance reveals the truth. If the surface drags or splinters, leave it on the hanger. Seek {texture_good_1} for slips and blouses softened until the sheen turns to candlelight. Rely on {texture_good_2} for jumpers and cardigans that insulate without bulk. Look for {texture_good_3} for dresses that trace the body with fluid, hidden depth. Finally, anchor your outerwear in {texture_good_4} for unstructured protective coats that stay soft at every seam. Memorise this material hierarchy.

Break any dark column with a {core_colour_3} layer, an {accent_colour_1} scarf, or an {accent_colour_2} belt. This strict chromatic discipline constructs your soft shelter.

Let a heavy silk crepe shirt act as your quiet sanctuary beneath a structured blazer. This keeps your approach self-contained, while a private silver pendant rests completely out of sight. Walking outside through freezing fog demands the exact same standard of materials as a formal presentation. Never downgrade your fibres for a minor task. Pair deep teal boiled wool trousers with dove grey washed silk on a routine morning, just as you would for a major pitch. The standard never drops simply because the context changes. Rely on the cool, matt finish of these garments to maintain your boundaries.

pattern_narrative.text: Let pattern provide hidden depth. Keep your contrast strictly tonal and low. Tailored garments must draw their markings directly from the weave of the loom. Fluid garments take their prints from {recommended_pattern_1} and {recommended_pattern_2}. Perform the squint check. Narrow your eyes until your eyelashes blur your vision. If the motif melts into a unified texture, keep the piece. If it flashes sharply and demands attention, leave it behind. Pass over stark borders, high-contrast graphics, {avoid_pattern_1}, and {avoid_pattern_2}. Every print must sink seamlessly into your cool colour palette.
pattern_narrative.sectionIntro: Master the rules of pattern to control the volume of your wardrobe.
tests: step back and soften focus (blur into texture)

pattern_tip.text: Evaluate {recommended_pattern_1} from across the room. Drape the garment over a chair and retreat to the doorway. Using only your peripheral vision, ensure the print dissolves into pure, matt texture. If the borders of the shapes remain crisp and legible, abandon the item. You require soft shelter. The correct piece will settle into a muted, cool wash of {core_colour_1} from afar, reserving its hidden depth for your own private inspection. Trace the raised threads with your thumbnail to confirm the physical reality of the weave. If the visual quietness matches the tactile density, acquire it.
pattern_tip.sectionIntro: Use this distance test to verify that your chosen prints remain private and illegible.
tests: step back and soften focus (blur into texture)

venus_cancer__moon_gemini__air_dominant.coreFormula: soft shelter + light movement + one sentimental keepsake
venus_cancer__moon_gemini__air_dominant.closing: Let your hands gauge the tension of the weave and your eyes confirm the clarity of the cut, grounding your personal instinct in soft shelter + light movement + one sentimental keepsake.

style_core.text:
Train your eye to look for clean separation. A garment must maintain its own edge without bleeding into the layer beneath it. Evaluate the cut by dropping the piece over your shoulders and watching how it settles. If the hem collapses or the sleeve loses its shape, leave the item on the rail. Apply the combination test to everything you buy. Put on two items and check the boundary where they meet. They must sit distinctly apart and retain a crisp edge. You want precise lines that trace the frame without clinging. The items must breathe and hold their structure intact.

Your defining equation is soft shelter + light movement + one sentimental keepsake. Picture a fluid slip skimming the torso, layered under an oversized woven wrap, finished with an inherited pendant resting on the collarbone. Keep the overall temperature cool and ensure the finish remains entirely muted. Build your collection slowly over time, scrutinising every seam. Once the pieces are ready, deploy your choices rapidly each morning. Allow your mood to dictate the final pairing.
style_core.sectionIntro: Memorise this blueprint, as it forms the non-negotiable logic behind every outfit you assemble.
tests: the combination test

textures_good.text: Build your foundation on fabrics that provide a soft shelter against the elements. Start by handling {texture_good_1} for shirting that maintains clean architectural boundaries. Layer this over {texture_good_2} for close-to-body base layers that offer sharp lines. Rely on {texture_good_3} for trousers that drape cleanly and resist creasing through a long day. Finish with {texture_good_4} as mobile outerwear to block the wind and guarantee light movement. Apply the snap test to every new garment. Pull the fabric taut between your hands and feel for a crisp recoil. A sluggish return means the weave lacks the structural tension required to anchor your cool palette and matt finishes. Let the textile's immediate bounce guide your purchasing decisions. The materials ranked below will consistently pass this physical threshold.
textures_good.sectionIntro: Learn to evaluate a garment by its touch before you ever consult a mirror.

rankedItems.useCase: shirting with clean architectural boundaries and pure separation

rankedItems.name: Fine ribbed merino
rankedItems.useCase: close-to-body base layers with sharp lines and easy movement

rankedItems.name: Worsted wool
rankedItems.useCase: trousers that drape cleanly and resist creasing through a long day

rankedItems.useCase: transitional jackets with rigid structure and no heavy drag
