pattern_tip.text: Apply the Distance Blur Test. Hang the garment on a door and step five paces back. Relax your eyes until your vision softens. A successful {recommended_pattern_1} must lose its sharp edges and melt into a warm texture. If the motif stays rigid or demands immediate focus, abandon the garment. You require prints that respect the garment cut while delivering continuous sensory comfort. An excellent pattern reads as a single block from across the shop floor and only reveals individual elements when held in your hands. Trust this shift in perception.

venus_sagittarius__moon_pisces__water_do.coreFormula: expansive colour + blurred edges + one theatrical finish
venus_sagittarius__moon_pisces__water_do.closing: Always let your tactile instincts govern the wardrobe, anchoring your daily uniform in expansive colour + blurred edges + one theatrical finish.

style_core.text:
Store mirrors lie. Rely instead on breath and flow. When evaluating a new garment, execute the host test. Put the piece on, drop your arms, and exhale completely. Notice how the fabric settles against your body. A successful item will float and drift in harmony with your natural posture. If the material clings tightly or builds a rigid cage around your joints, reject it immediately. Look for textiles that behave like quiet, moving air. Curate your collection slowly over the months, but combine the pieces with swift daily instinct.

Your structural foundation requires precise balance, defined by this exact equation: expansive colour + blurred edges + one theatrical finish. Picture a generous, matt cashmere overcoat in a muted, warm rust. Layer it over soft, washed silk trousers that lack sharp creases. Add a single, oversized brass and silver cuff. The silhouette succeeds because the garments breathe and interact without stiff boundaries. Every choice must respect this need for gentle movement. Trust your hands to feel the drape. A worthy piece always yields to your form.
style_core.sectionIntro: Mastering your core style requires strict physical tests and a reliable formula.

textures_good.text: Your foundation demands materials that anchor warm tones and create blurred edges. Introduce the melt test to every potential purchase. Crush the fabric in your fist. If it springs back with rigid resistance, leave the shop. True quality must yield and dissolve entirely into your palm. Look for {texture_good_1} in slips and blouses softened until the sheen mimics dim candlelight. Choose {texture_good_2} for jumpers and cardigans that shelter without adding bulk. Rely on {texture_good_3} for dresses that trace the body with fluid, hidden depth. Finish with {texture_good_4} for unstructured protective coats that remain completely supple at the hem. Hunt down these specific fibres to guarantee a matt, grounded base.
textures_good.sectionIntro: Build your wardrobe from materials that submit completely to your touch.

It catches the light with a hard glare that shatters a muted, matt palette. Stiff synthetics refuse to drape. They create harsh shadows that clash with a warm aesthetic. If the textile feels like brittle paper or cold plastic, leave it on the rail.

Your aesthetic thrives on this yielding quality, grounded in warm tones and punctuated by one theatrical finish. Look for matt surfaces that actively absorb the ambient room lighting. Rely on this gravitational drop to weed out unyielding, structured synthetics.

palette_narrative.text: Your palette runs strictly warm. Build your foundation by combining {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_2} as your daily base, grounding them with {core_colour_3} and {core_colour_4} for structural weight. Introduce {accent_colour_1} or {accent_colour_2} to anchor the look. Always pass over stark optic white, harsh neon, and icy pastel. Apply the vein check. Hold the fabric directly against the inside of your wrist. An incorrect dye will hover aggressively above the skin, while the right pigment sinks in and harmonises. Avoid the visual sinkhole. Stacking flat, matt base tones without a textural break swallows your frame completely. Fix this heaviness by slipping a lighter woven collar near the neck or adding one piece of heavy, brushed metal to crack the uniform surface.

occasions_work.text: At the desk, build an unbroken column of warm bitter chocolate or dark mahogany. Support this base by softening your garment boundaries. Select heavy silk crepe that falls gently over the collarbone instead of a stiffly ironed cotton shirt. Provide one theatrical finish at the wrist using mixed metals in a completely matt finish. Apply the chair spin test. Swivel away from your monitor and notice how the trousers move around your knees. The fabric must drape smoothly without catching. A midday dash down to the street for an espresso does not demand dropping your balanced column for a shapeless fleece. Give preference to textiles with significant density that pool rather than peak, preserving those vital blurred edges.

Overhead lamplight turns rigid lines harsh. Keep the drape intact without introducing sharpness. Execute the shoulder pinch. Grab the excess material at your collar and let it drop. It must settle perfectly matt and flush against the body. Choose dark mahogany boiled wool over structured outerwear for late dinners. Mix silver and gold chains without hesitation, ensuring every piece remains completely muted. If a metallic surface catches the indoor glow with a hard glare, remove it immediately.

Walk briskly across the room and ensure the cloth sways without clinging to your calves. Mix gold and silver rings freely against a dark mahogany sleeve. Maintain your strict standard across every ordinary hour. Your absolute requirement remains expansive colour + blurred edges + one theatrical finish. If a jacket feels rigid across the back or demands constant adjustment, leave it behind. Your daily clothes must support physical action entirely without friction.

A highly polished, reflective metal cuts too sharply across soft, warm palettes. Prevent this jarring effect by anchoring your pieces exclusively in matt and brushed textures to maintain your blurred edges. Reject any piece with a {excluded_finish} treatment. Apply the palm drop. Hold the necklace an inch above your open hand and let go. Visuals deceive, but an instant, heavy gravitational impact confirms true quality. A dense, brushed buckle grounds your garments, while hollow, lightweight components destroy the balance.

style_core.text:
Assess garments by their draft and flow. Watch how the fabric moves across your ribs and settles over your shoulders. Does it sweep with a soft exhale, or does it stifle you? If it clings rigidly, drop it. Build a wardrobe that breathes with you. Assemble an outfit to prove it. Take an enveloping warm knit, pair it with fluid, floor-skimming woven trousers, and finish the waist with a heavy, polished buckle. That sequence delivers your exact result: quiet grandeur + hidden depth + one molten flash. Every piece must glide.

Construct this collection patiently over time. Accumulate individual pieces slowly, then deploy them rapidly when dressing. Your final arbiter is the wrist test. When trying on a sleeve, drop your arm to your side and let it hang. Study the cloth pooling at your cuff. It must cascade softly over your skin, never catching or dragging as you move. If the cuff grips tightly or refuses to sway when you rotate your hand, the garment fails. Trust your eyes to read the current of the fabric.

venus_capricorn__moon_pisces__fire_domin.coreFormula: enduring structure + blurred edges + one living texture
venus_capricorn__moon_pisces__fire_domin.closing: Your style guide is a map of your own instincts, built on enduring structure + blurred edges + one living texture.

style_core.text:
Assess every item by its momentum. Put the piece on and walk rapidly across the room. If it tangles, pulls, or slows your step, abandon it. Call it the stride check. You are looking for pieces that keep pace with your physical velocity rather than dragging you backward. A dense hem that catches on your calves or a sleeve that restricts your reach will fail you. Trust the stride check over a mirror. Curate the collection slowly over months, dress fast in the morning, and let your choices drift by mood. When the pieces match your speed, you never hesitate before leaving.

The foundation of this quiet approach lies in your strict styling equation. You will operate using enduring structure + blurred edges + one living texture as your constant baseline. Picture a sharply tailored wool coat sitting over a diffuse silk shell, grounded by a supple leather belt. The crisp lines of the tailoring map out the boundaries, the silk softens the interior, and the leather breathes subtle life into the combination. Keep the metals warm and the overall palette muted to ensure nothing interrupts the eye. Rely entirely on items that let you move forward without friction.
style_core.sectionIntro: This is your foundational strategy for building a wardrobe that operates at your exact speed.

Soften the interior with warm {texture_good_4} for linings and blouses that rest against the skin, and rely on {texture_good_1} for knitwear that compresses and recovers its shape. This is how you introduce blurred edges and one living texture into a self-contained wardrobe. Before acquiring any new piece, perform the breath test. Press the material directly over your mouth and exhale. If the air traps and bounces back, the fabric will suffocate you. Leave it on the rack. Rely on your bodily response. A well-made garment supports your internal heat rather than smothering it. Here are the textures that pass.

textures_bad.text: Apply the scratch test to any prospective cloth before buying. Drag your fingernails gently across the weave. If the yarns snag or squeak, the material fails. Drop it. {texture_bad_1} feels aggressively slick against the hands and refuses to yield. It sits rigidly rather than draping, completely disrupting your blurred edges. {texture_bad_2} locks the body inside a harsh, unyielding shell. Keep your fingers tightly attuned to the matt finish of every textile. If {texture_bad_3} catches the light with a cheap sheen or feels like a plastic film, leave it on the rail. You require materials that absorb light and quietly contain your warm temperature, never rigid synthetic traps.
textures_bad.sectionIntro: Train your hands to reject materials that fight your self-contained warmth.
tests: the breath test

textures_sweet_spot.text: Your material sweet spot requires {sweet_spot_keyword_1} layered with {sweet_spot_keyword_2}. Combine these to build enduring structure. Run your bare hand over {texture_good_1}. Seek warm, matt finishes that remain entirely self-contained. Protect your space by executing the stretch-and-release check. Pull the fabric gently between your thumbs and let go. The cloth must spring back into shape immediately without bagging. If a material stays deformed, leave it on the rack. Flimsy knits warp and lose their perimeter. Prioritise fabrics that maintain their own firm boundaries naturally.
textures_sweet_spot.sectionIntro: Master the precise material baseline that defines your wardrobe, and refuse to compromise on how cloth behaves.
tests: the breath test

palette_narrative.text: Maintain a warm temperature to ground every choice. Form enduring structure using {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_2} as your dense foundation. Shift to {core_colour_3} and {core_colour_4} when you need a lighter bridge between heavy layers. Deploy {accent_colour_1} or {accent_colour_2} as your single deliberate accent. When a silhouette feels too rigid, reach for your relief tone to introduce breathing space. Always pass over icy electric blue, neon brights, and fleeting trend pastels. Watch out for the dense sink. Stacking heavy, flat tones without a break can swallow you whole. Lift the weight by placing a {core_colour_3} layer near the face, adding a piece of supple leather, or breaking the block with your relief tone.
palette_narrative.sectionIntro: Your palette is a physical tool to control visual weight, not a decoration.

traps.failure: the all-earth sink: brown on olive on bark, all matt and all heavy, can swallow you whole
traps.fix: lift it: an oatmeal cream layer at the collar, one piece of polished leather, or the washed blue relief

occasions_work.text: At the desk, let enduring structure take the lead. A heavy camel coat or a strict wool trouser provides the necessary boundary for a self-contained day. Support this firm outline with blurred edges near the face. Use a matt silk blouse or a brushed scarf in a warm, rich toffee to soften the geometry. Stepping out for a midday coffee does not require shedding your tailored silhouette entirely. Keep the heavy overcoat on, even if the underlayers relax. Anchor the look with one living texture, like an aged leather belt with a brass buckle that grounds the composition. Trust your own instincts as your primary guide. Appearance deceives, but the physical heft of a garment tells the truth.
occasions_work.sectionIntro: Navigate your working hours by letting your silhouette hold the line.
tests: a trip for milk does not require abandoning your silhouette (Tide)

occasions_intimate.text: In close, low-light settings, your blurred edges take the lead. Dim lighting swallows sharp cuts. Rely on the physical heft of heavy silk or the dense pile of brushed wool to cast deep shadows. Pick up a rich toffee overshirt. Apply the blind drape test. Close your eyes and let the fabric pool in your open hands. Assess the garment by its actual mass. Moving from a restaurant to a damp street requires immediate insulation. Maintain your perimeter with a warm camel cashmere knit that sits close to the collarbone. Keep all finishes strictly matt to deaden the ambient glow. If the weave feels thin and flimsy, drop it.
occasions_intimate.sectionIntro: Calibrate your evening wear by testing every piece strictly by touch.
tests: a trip for milk does not require abandoning your silhouette (Tide)

occasions_daily.text: Running casual errands is no reason to abandon your foundation. A Tuesday morning trip to the chemist demands the exact same equation as an evening appointment. Demand total consistency. You must execute enduring structure + blurred edges + one living texture, every single day. Pull on a heavy camel cashmere knit over straight-leg brushed wool trousers. Anchor the foot with a warm toffee full-grain leather boot. The garments must hold their own shape before they touch your skin. Grip the knitwear in your fist. The yarn must possess enough internal density to support the shoulder line without drooping. If a piece collapses under its own mass, drop it back into the drawer.
occasions_daily.sectionIntro: Anchor your mornings in the exact same discipline you apply to formal appointments.
tests: a trip for milk does not require abandoning your silhouette (Tide)

hardware_metals.text: Focus your hardware on a warm palette. Base your jewellery and functional clasps on {metal_1} and {metal_2}. These tones provide enduring structure without breaking the quiet continuity of your wardrobe. Rely on your own grip to judge quality. Lift every piece to gauge its density. Evaluate metal strictly by its physical drag in your palm. Reject highly polished materials. High-shine {excluded_finish} creates a sharp, distracting glare that clashes with soft, muted garments. Fix this by demanding matt and brushed textures exclusively. A brushed {metal_3} watch case or a matt belt buckle drinks the light, keeping the entire look grounded and self-contained.
hardware_metals.sectionIntro: Select your hardware as strictly as you choose your heaviest winter coat.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

hardware_stones.text: When selecting stones, demand opaque depth and warm resonance. Execute the chill check. Press a {stone_1} or {stone_2} cabochon against your cheek. A genuine mineral holds a sharp initial cold before drawing your body heat. Judge a setting by this thermal exchange. Anchor your wardrobe with this authentic substance, ensuring the jewellery acts as an enduring structure against your soft wools. Shun faceted cuts that throw scattered sparks across a room. A sparkling gemstone feels jagged and intensely distracting next to your muted garments. Stick to smooth, rounded domes to keep the visual temperature cohesive and self-contained.
hardware_stones.sectionIntro: Test and select hardware that anchors your silhouette without screaming for attention.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

hardware_tip.text: Perform the tap test on any warm {metal_1} before purchase. Strike the clasp or link gently against a wooden table. You are listening for a dull, heavy thud. A solid cast piece provides enduring structure, whereas a hollow item produces a cheap, tinny rattle. Flee from any mirrored plating. A reflective surface slices roughly across your quiet textures and ruins the continuity. Seek out a dark, oxidised patina to protect your blurred edges. The metal must nestle into the weave of your clothing, rather than glaring outward.
hardware_tip.sectionIntro: Evaluate your hardware by weight and finish.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

accessory_1.text: Treat your accessories as the final provider of one living texture. They must ground the blurred edges of a soft layer and reinforce the enduring structure of your tailoring. Restrict yourself to one or two strong pieces per look. Begin with handbags. Demand full-grain leather or thick suede fitted with matt brushed hardware. Apply the crease check. Bend the flap of a leather satchel back on itself. A rich hide develops a lighter tone at the fold, proving its natural grain. If the surface remains stiff and entirely uniform like plastic, leave it behind. Trust the rich scent and pliable resistance of untreated skins to confirm their quality.
accessory_1.sectionIntro: Select your finishing elements with strict, physical discipline.

accessory_2.text: Seek out generous silk or fine wool for your scarves. Choose only matt finishes that refuse to shine. Perform the slip test. Knot the fabric loosely around your neck and tug the ends. A superior yarn slides smoothly over itself without catching or snapping. You need this generous, fluid volume to supply blurred edges against tailored outerwear. Look for a single strike of rich toffee glowing deep within a muted background, resembling a solitary warm coal buried in ash. Shun stark graphic logos. Let the piece remain self-contained, a private layer of insulation rather than a public billboard.
accessory_2.sectionIntro: Dictate the final shape of your silhouette by selecting fabrics that respond strictly to gravity.

accessory_3.text: Provide enduring structure with a wide, thick leather belt. Inspect the edge before threading it through your loops. An unpainted, burnished raw edge indicates true craftsmanship, while a sealed plastic rim betrays a cheap composite. For keepsake jewellery, select a deep cabochon set in darkened brass. Execute the solitary flame rule. Before stepping out, ensure only one point of warm heat glows against your muted garments. If a second pendant or heavily studded cuff tries to compete, strip the extra item away. Keep the focus entirely self-contained.
accessory_3.sectionIntro: Select your final hardware and apply a strict boundary to your focal points.

pattern_narrative.text: Enduring structure in your tailoring demands patterns woven directly into the loom. Look for a tonal {recommended_pattern_1} or a low-contrast {recommended_pattern_2}. Fluid garments tolerate dyed motifs, but you must keep the transitions strictly muted. Pass over {avoid_pattern_1} and {avoid_pattern_2} without a second thought. Trust your physical instincts over visual noise. Apply the shadow check. Walk the garment away from the shop windows. The pattern must merge and turn exceptionally warm in low light. If the contrast remains stark and jarring away from the sun, drop it. Demand blurred edges where the threads meet. You are wearing quiet, matt shifts of shade.
pattern_narrative.sectionIntro: Pattern is an architectural decision rather than a decorative afterthought.
tests: the firelight test (deepen and warm by lamplight)

pattern_tip.text: Subject any {recommended_pattern_1} to the squint test before bringing it home. Narrow your eyes until your eyelashes blur the room. The individual shapes must dissolve instantly into a single warm field of colour. If the lines remain distinctly aggressive and refuse to blend, leave the garment on the hanger. Seek out motifs that sink gracefully into the background. This optical melt guarantees the blurred edges necessary for your privacy. High-contrast prints demand constant attention. A truly self-contained pattern keeps its secrets close.
pattern_tip.sectionIntro: Subject every pattern to a strict environmental lighting check.
tests: the firelight test (deepen and warm by lamplight)

venus_taurus__moon_capricorn__earth_domi.coreFormula: structure + softness + a touch of quiet depth
venus_taurus__moon_capricorn__earth_domi.closing: Your style guide is a map of your own instincts, built on structure + softness + a touch of quiet depth.

style_core.text:
Your fingers are your most reliable diagnostic tools. When you evaluate a new item, close your eyes and feel its density. A worthy piece feels substantial and honest under your fingers. Let physical weight point you toward quality. Curate this collection deliberately over seasons, but pull it on in seconds each morning. Before you buy anything new, apply the decade test. Ask yourself if the cut and drape will still make perfect sense ten winters from now. If you hesitate, leave it on the rack.

Your guiding equation is structure + softness + a touch of quiet depth. Picture this balance in action. Imagine a sharply tailored heavy wool overcoat sitting over a fluid cashmere rollneck, finished with a muted, heavy pendant. The coat provides rigid architecture. The knit brings a warm, yielding drape. The small accessory offers subtle dimension without demanding attention. Rely on this self-contained approach. You dress for your own satisfaction, never for an audience. When an outfit feels perfectly balanced, trust your math.
style_core.sectionIntro: Master your foundational logic to build a wardrobe that serves you.
tests: the decade test

textures_good.text: Your wardrobe demands materials that build structure while maintaining absolute softness. Depend on your fingers to find this equilibrium. When evaluating a new piece, execute the candlelight test. Hold the fabric in dim, warm light and watch how the matt surface absorbs illumination to reveal a touch of quiet depth. Avoid any finish that glints or distracts. Look for {texture_good_3} for tailored coats and blazers that hold clean lines for years. Anchor these outer shells with {texture_good_1} for knitwear that compresses and bounces back against the palm. Keep {texture_good_4} close to the skin for blouses and linings that add fluid movement. Finish the foundation with {texture_good_2} for bags and belts that mould to your shape and patinate over time. Master this specific list.
textures_good.sectionIntro: Train your hands to recognise the correct physical weight before a garment ever touches your skin.

rankedItems.name: Cashmere
rankedItems.useCase: knitwear and winter layers that compress and bounce back

rankedItems.useCase: bags, shoes, and belts that mould to your life and patinate

rankedItems.useCase: tailored coats and blazers that hold clean lines for years

textures_bad.text: Avoid {texture_bad_1}. Squeeze the material in your fist. If it springs back with a brittle, synthetic resistance, it fails the skin. You require garments that grant true structure and softness, never a rigid cage. Stay away from {texture_bad_2}. Run your thumb across the grain. If the weave feels slick or hollow, it will never hold a touch of quiet depth. Test the fabric's friction against itself. If the surface snags or throws off a harsh glare under direct light instead of settling into a rich, matt shadow, drop the item entirely. Rely on bodily feedback over visual appeal. Sight can be tricked, but tactile mass never falters.
textures_bad.sectionIntro: Learn to reject materials that betray your physical instincts.
tests: the candlelight test

textures_sweet_spot.text: Combine {sweet_spot_keyword_1} and {sweet_spot_keyword_2}. Take a piece of {texture_good_1} into a shadowed corner. The material must absorb the ambient, warm glow and remain entirely matt. If it shines, walk away. A heavily muted cloth secures a touch of quiet depth and keeps the silhouette strictly self-contained. Rely on dense threads that hold a clean line without making a sound. Keep your layers heavy and silent.
textures_sweet_spot.sectionIntro: Master your tactile baseline by merging two distinct weaves.
tests: the candlelight test

palette_narrative.text: Assess every garment by its temperature. Your palette is decisively warm. Anchor your foundation in {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_2}, then bridge these heavy bases using {core_colour_3} and {core_colour_4}. Apply {accent_colour_1} or {accent_colour_2} only for a deliberate focal point. Treat your relief tone as a subtle note used sparingly to break up heavy layers. Reject icy electric blue, neon brights, and fleeting trend pastels. Beware the trap of metallic excess, where too much shiny hardware turns an imposing silhouette into a cheap display. Reflections lie, but gravitational pull always tells the truth. Let your bodily reaction to heavy chains warn you. Anchor your look with a single gleaming element. Demand that all remaining hardware stays tiny, matt, and strictly in the background. This exact discipline secures structure, softness, and a touch of quiet depth.
palette_narrative.sectionIntro: Master your palette to build an unshakable foundation.

traps.failure: gilt overload: grandeur tips into gaudiness
traps.fix: hold the line at one molten flash and keep every other metal moment small, matt, and supporting

occasions_work.text: At work, structure leads. Build your outfit around a camel brushed wool blazer or crisp trousers. Let the tailoring hold your shape. Clearing your inbox does not require abandoning your form for a shapeless pullover. Once you establish the rigid lines, introduce a rich toffee silk blouse or a fine cashmere crewneck to provide softness. This yielding layer sits close to the body. Finish the outfit with a touch of quiet depth. Add a matt gold signet ring next to a brushed silver cuff. These heavy, warm pieces anchor the garments.
occasions_work.sectionIntro: Let your firm tailoring lead the way at the office, allowing your yielding fabrics to play a supporting role.
tests: paying a utility bill does not require collapsing your silhouette (Blaze)

occasions_intimate.text: A touch of quiet depth leads in dim settings. When proximity closes in, the eye yields to the hand. Let brushed wool and camel cashmere do the heavy lifting in a close room. Keep the palette temperature warm. Swap crisp daytime garments for a rich toffee silk shirt that absorbs ambient light rather than reflecting it. Ensure every textile remains matt. Mixed metal jewellery provides the only gleam. Retain your form even behind closed doors. Paying a late utility bill at the kitchen table does not require collapsing your frame into a slouchy dressing gown. Maintain your self-contained boundaries. Keep the structure in your shoulders with a dense knit, then introduce softness through the drape of the sleeve.
occasions_intimate.sectionIntro: Calibrate your garments for close proximity and low light.
tests: paying a utility bill does not require collapsing your silhouette (Blaze)

occasions_daily.text: Treat your mundane routines as the testing ground for your discipline. Going to the local pharmacy for paracetamol is no excuse to swap tailored edges for sagging joggers. Disputing a council tax bill at the post office demands no collapse of your silhouette into unstructured cotton. Maintain your boundaries. Pick up a warm camel cashmere jumper instead of a hoodie. Pair it with a matt gold chain, polished silver rings, and a brushed wool coat. Everything you put on your body must serve your internal standard. Your baseline never suspends itself for mere errands. Whether you are buying milk or reviewing documents at home, the daily mandate remains exactly the same. You always need structure + softness + a touch of quiet depth.
occasions_daily.sectionIntro: Enforce your personal standard across every mundane errand.
tests: paying a utility bill does not require collapsing your silhouette (Blaze)

hardware_metals.text: Your approach to metal operates on two strict tracks. Reserve {personal_metal_1} and {personal_metal_2} for jewellery resting directly against your skin. Their warm tones anchor your palette. For clasps, zips, and buckles, rely entirely on {structural_metal_1} and {structural_metal_2}. This separation provides clean structure without competing focal points. Every piece of hardware must pass a physical check. Lift the component in your bare palm. Ignore the showroom lighting. Actual mass reveals the truth. Reject hollow alloys. Highly polished finishes catch the light sharply and distract from understated garments. Fix this error by sticking exclusively to matt and brushed textures to keep the outfit cohesive. Ban {excluded_finish} entirely.
hardware_metals.sectionIntro: Establish strict boundaries between the metals resting on your skin and the hardware securing your garments.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

hardware_stones.text: Press the stone against your inner wrist to test its thermal mass. Judge the mineral by how quickly it absorbs your body heat. Seek out dense, opaque options like {stone_1} or {stone_2} that hold warmth inside rather than flashing it outward. A highly polished stone or a glaringly reflective metal creates a jarring distraction against your muted fabrics. Anchor your hardware with completely matt or gently brushed finishes to maintain visual cohesion. These materials provide the firm structure required to ground the inherent softness of your garments. Keep the scale contained and entirely deliberate.
hardware_stones.sectionIntro: Evaluate your stones by their density and their capacity to absorb ambient light.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

hardware_tip.text: Assess closures and chains by their drag against your thumb. Hardware must carry enough literal gravity to anchor the softness of your garments with grounded structure. A flimsy zip betrays the whole outfit. Watch out for blinding reflections. An overly gleaming edge clashes against warm, muted fabrics, breaking the visual cohesion. Stick strictly to brushed or matt {metal_1} to keep the integration seamless. Evaluate every buckle or clasp independently. Give the mechanism a sharp tug. If the item lacks substance, discard the entire garment.
hardware_tip.sectionIntro: Apply this physical test to evaluate hardware before bringing it into your wardrobe.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

accessory_1.text: You have anchored your garments in structure and softness. Now, use accessories to deliver a touch of quiet depth. Limit yourself to one or two strong pieces per look. Subject every item to the heirloom test. Assess whether the object possesses the dense, warm permanence required to be passed down decades from now. Rely on the uncompromising reality of physical materials. Apply this strict standard to handbags. Demand full-grain leather or thick suede. Inspect the metal closures and reject anything highly polished. Accept only matt, brushed hardware. Genuine density confirms quality.
accessory_1.sectionIntro: Apply the same strict standards to your accessories as you do your garments, forcing every piece to earn its place through sheer physical weight.

accessory_2.text: Treat your scarves as the final layer of your self-contained presentation. Choose generous squares of matt silk or lengths of fine, warm wool. These textiles bridge the strict lines of tailored structure with pure, tactile softness. Scrutinise the hand-rolled edges and the density of the weave. Grip the material and verify it possesses the integrity to outlive you, remaining immaculate enough to wrap in tissue for the next generation. If the textile feels flimsy or the surface catches too much light, abandon the purchase. Seek only muted, substantial fabrics in deep camel or rich ochre that ground your silhouette.

For keepsake jewellery, seek out a deep stone set in oxidised silver or aged brass. These heavy metals ground your warm camel layers. Before you purchase anything new, apply the legacy check. Close your eyes and measure the metal against your collarbone. Ask yourself if it mimics a hefty artefact uncovered in a wooden box inherited decades ago. If the item feels hollow and shines like thin foil, drop it and walk away.

pattern_narrative.text: Establish structure in your tailored garments by sourcing pattern entirely from the physical weave of the cloth. Allow pieces that deliver softness to carry {recommended_pattern_1} or {recommended_pattern_2}. Keep all internal contrast strictly low and tonal to preserve a muted finish. Always verify the underlying base remains warm. Ignore {avoid_pattern_1} entirely. Pass over anything resembling {avoid_pattern_2}. Subject every new print to the landscape test. The motifs must merge into a continuous surface, mimicking the natural grain of earth observed from a high hill. Trust your fingers to evaluate the woven cloth before your eyes assess the print. The eye skips details, but the hand confirms reality.
pattern_narrative.sectionIntro: Dictate your prints with strict boundaries to build texture rather than demanding attention.

pattern_tip.text: To execute the landscape test, drape the garment across a chair and step back. Blur your focus until {recommended_pattern_1} loses its distinct boundaries. The print must settle into a continuous, organic surface. Picture the terrain seen from a high ridge. You want the overall effect of a warm, distant vista. If the shapes pop out or refuse to blend, reject the piece. You require a pattern that provides internal structure without sacrificing visual softness. It must retreat into a unified texture rather than projecting outwards. Trust your own perception to recognise this shift. If the motif reads clearly from across the room, bypass the garment.
pattern_tip.sectionIntro: Master the field test to evaluate any pattern before you bring it into your wardrobe.
tests: the field test (settle into a natural texture like ground seen from a hill)

venus_libra__moon_virgo__water_dominant.coreFormula: balanced proportions + honest fabric + one refined touch
venus_libra__moon_virgo__water_dominant.closing: Let every sartorial choice begin with your physical instincts and end with balanced proportions + honest fabric + one refined touch.

style_core.text:
Assemble your wardrobe slowly, but drift by mood when you pull items together each morning. The physical sensation of a garment against your skin dictates everything. Pay attention to how a piece moves and settles. Does it breathe with you, or does it stifle? Apply the drift test. Hold an item up and drop it onto a flat surface. Watch how it catches the air. If it sinks like a stone or collapses stiffly, leave it on the rail. You need pieces that float down and settle gracefully, proving they will skim your shape rather than cling.

Memorise your guiding structure. Every successful outfit requires balanced proportions + honest fabric + one refined touch. Picture a fluid button-down shirt tucked into a wide straight trouser, finished with a single piece of cool metal at the collar. The shirt gives you airflow, the trouser provides structural weight at the hem, and the subtle accessory anchors the entire look. Trust your hands to check the drape before you put anything on. If the material passes the drift test and aligns with your mixed finishes, it belongs in your collection.
style_core.sectionIntro: Master your foundational style logic and learn exactly how to physically test your clothes before you buy them.
tests: the flight test

textures_good.text: Evaluate a material by how it settles. Execute the forearm drape. Toss the garment over your bare arm and watch the descent. You need materials that settle into balanced proportions instead of freezing into rigid shapes. Anchor your cool colours using {texture_good_1} for slips and blouses softened until the sheen turns to candlelight. Seek out {texture_good_2} for jumpers and cardigans that shelter without weight. Choose {texture_good_3} for dresses that trace the body with fluid, hidden depth. Build your outer layers with {texture_good_4} for unstructured protective coats that stay soft at every edge. An honest fabric performs instinctively. Rely on the priority list below.
textures_good.sectionIntro: Train your hands to recognise honest fabric before you even look at the garment tag.

textures_bad.text: Grab {texture_bad_1} and crush it in your fist. If it squeaks, holds sharp creases, or lacks a cool touch against your skin, drop it instantly. Your body demands materials that yield. Fling {texture_bad_2} across a wide table to gauge its density. If the item hovers stiffly like a plastic bag, leave it on the rack. Superior yarns cascade cleanly, while cheap synthetics trap air awkwardly. Your style relies on one refined touch, a finish you can never achieve over a foundation that feels rigid or artificial. Squeeze every fibre. If the textile fights your grip, walk away.
textures_bad.sectionIntro: Learn to recognise imposters before they touch your skin.
tests: the flight test

textures_sweet_spot.text: Merge {sweet_spot_keyword_1} with {sweet_spot_keyword_2}. Take an item cut from {texture_good_1} and pack it tightly into a sphere. Ask yourself if it can survive ten hours crammed into a cabin bag and emerge wearable. If the cloth crushes permanently, leave it on the rail. An honest fabric works hard and recovers fast. Contrast a matt finish against a faint sheen to build visual depth. Keep the temperature cool. You need surfaces that perform under stress without demanding delicate care.
textures_sweet_spot.sectionIntro: Master the interaction between two surfaces to lock in your working wardrobe.
tests: the flight test

palette_narrative.text: Keep your temperature entirely cool. Build your base from {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_3}. Deploy {core_colour_2} for structural weight, and substitute stark white with {core_colour_4} to keep transitions smooth. Reserve {accent_colour_1} and {accent_colour_2} as your single deliberate accent. Apply your relief tone when your eye needs empty space between heavy garments. Pass over hard black, harsh neon, and heavy earth brown. Fold the sleeve sharply under a bright lamp. If the crease casts a chalky shadow, put it back. You need an honest fabric to carry these shades properly. Beware the defensive outerwear trap. When your schedule accelerates, you might instinctively grab stiff technical jackets. Leave those unyielding shells behind. Trade that harsh exterior for a dense brushed cotton overshirt that matches your rapid pace and provides genuine support. Maintain balanced proportions by placing darker tones where you need visual grounding.
palette_narrative.sectionIntro: Anchor your wardrobe in this strict system of tones to guarantee every item pulls its weight.

traps.failure: the hard shell: reaching for armour, technical jackets, and stiff utility layers when life speeds up
traps.fix: swap the shell for a brushed cotton layer that moves just as fast and holds you besides

occasions_work.text: At the office, let balanced proportions lead. A heavy silk crepe blouse tucked into wide-leg trousers sets a clear structural standard. Let an honest fabric act as the primary support system for this shape. Choose washed silk or boiled wool for their tactile integrity rather than delicate finishes. Finally, bring in one refined touch to complete the profile. Before commuting, apply the downpour check. Ask yourself if you would wear these exact trousers to fetch the morning paper in the pouring rain. If they demand precious treatment or restrict movement, take them off. Office clothing must offer the exact same yielding comfort as your most familiar weekend jumpers. Stick to cool tones like dove grey and deep teal to set a calm visual baseline.
occasions_work.sectionIntro: Frame your office choices by establishing a clear structural hierarchy.
tests: a rainy Sunday paper run deserves the same gentle standard (Ripple)

occasions_intimate.text: In close, low-light settings, your honest fabric takes the lead. When shadows obscure shape, touch supersedes sight. Layer deep teal washed silk under a dove grey cloud-soft lambswool cardigan. Mix your jewellery freely to catch the dim lighting. Pair a matt platinum band with polished gold hoops and a dark sapphire pendant. Apply the blind touch standard. Evening attire requires the identical gentle ease as your Sunday morning layers. If heavy silk crepe feels restrictive during dinner, discard it for the night. Keep your palette firmly cool and maintain your balanced proportions. Trust physical sensation over your reflection. If a garment pinches, leave it in the wardrobe.
occasions_intimate.sectionIntro: Navigate intimate evening occasions by prioritising physical sensation over the mirror.
tests: a rainy Sunday paper run deserves the same gentle standard (Ripple)

occasions_daily.text: When you step out to run a sudden errand, demand the exact same standard of care as you would for a formal meeting. Pull on a dove grey boiled wool jumper rather than a fleece zip-up. Slip a matt platinum band onto your finger before grabbing the house keys. Your routine relies on a strict equation. Always execute balanced proportions + honest fabric + one refined touch every single time you cross the threshold. Let cool tones anchor these choices. Pair straight denim with a deep teal washed silk shirt for a rapid trip to the local pharmacy. Do not suspend your rules for mundane tasks.
occasions_daily.sectionIntro: Treat a routine Tuesday morning with the exactitude you bring to a scheduled appointment.
tests: a rainy Sunday paper run deserves the same gentle standard (Ripple)

hardware_metals.text: You hold total freedom to mix metals. Wear {metal_1} at your cuffs alongside a {metal_2} watch and a {metal_3} belt buckle. Blend highly polished elements with {excluded_finish} surfaces freely. Always test hardware by bouncing it gently in your hand. Vision deceives, but mass reveals the truth. A heavy, solid metal grounds your silhouette and ensures balanced proportions. Do not buy artificially aged pieces. Antiqued brass and hammered copper create visual clutter against a crisp wardrobe. Fix this by sticking strictly to polished silver, platinum, white gold, and mirror chrome. These clean, cool metals act as the necessary rigid counterpoint to an honest fabric.
hardware_metals.sectionIntro: Hardware provides the rigid anchor point for your entire wardrobe.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: antiqued brass, hammered copper, and artificially aged metal read as clutter against a present-tense style
traps.fix: keep to polished silver, platinum, white gold, and mirror chrome

hardware_stones.text: When adding gems to an honest fabric, let density dictate your choices. Press the mineral against your skin before you wear it. A heavy {stone_1} or a cold {stone_2} must feel like a grounded physical object against your collarbone. Steer clear of simulated vintage settings and faux-tarnish mounts. These elements register as historical clutter against a crisp, modern aesthetic. Set your stones in stark, unblemished alloys to keep the overall temperature cool. Mix these finishes freely across the body to maintain balanced proportions without looking rigid. Let the solid mass of a dense {stone_3} act as your one refined touch.
hardware_stones.sectionIntro: Evaluate your gems by their physical density and how they catch the light.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: antiqued brass, hammered copper, and artificially aged metal read as clutter against a present-tense style
traps.fix: keep to polished silver, platinum, white gold, and mirror chrome

hardware_tip.text: Assess accessories solely by their heft. Flick the edge of any {metal_1} or {stone_1} piece with your fingernail. If a ring or clasp sounds hollow or tinny, leave it on the shelf. You anchor your wardrobe with balanced proportions, so demand the exact same structural integrity from your accents. Reject intentionally distressed surfaces and dull, oxidised chains. These textures register as visual noise against a cool palette. Instead, confine your selections to gleaming, unblemished items. When you build a foundation of honest fabric, your finishing details must remain razor-sharp and perfectly clear.
hardware_tip.sectionIntro: Lift the piece to understand its true value.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: antiqued brass, hammered copper, and artificially aged metal read as clutter against a present-tense style
traps.fix: keep to polished silver, platinum, white gold, and mirror chrome

accessory_1.text: You have built the foundation with balanced proportions. You have anchored the silhouette in an honest fabric. Now you introduce one refined touch. Restrict your additions to one or two strong pieces per look. Put your completed outfit on and execute the subtraction rule. Remove a single item from your frame. If the structure holds its ground, leave that extra piece on the table. Begin your selection with silk scarves. Seek out a crepe de chine square with a deliberately matt surface. Trust your fingers as you manipulate the material. Feel the cool friction of the woven threads against your bare skin.
accessory_1.sectionIntro: Select and edit your finishing elements through strict physical testing.

rankedItems.name: Silk scarves
rankedItems.useCase: crepe de chine square, matt

tests: the take-off test

accessory_2.text: Build your fine jewellery collection from thin, mixed gold and silver pieces. Combine polished and brushed metals freely. This strategy relies on balanced proportions across your hands and neck. Wear a slender brushed gold chain with polished cool silver rings. Perform the acoustic check. Shake your wrist sharply. If the bands clink together constantly, remove a layer. Let the mixed finishes provide the visual friction instead of sheer volume. Do not let heavy metal slabs overwhelm an honest fabric like washed silk or boiled wool. The simple contrast of polished and brushed surfaces functions perfectly as one refined touch.
accessory_2.sectionIntro: Evaluate your secondary pieces to ensure they serve the whole.

rankedItems.name: Fine jewellery
rankedItems.useCase: thin mixed gold and silver, mixed polished and brushed

tests: the take-off test

accessory_3.text: Source a small structured bag. Pick a top-handle shape in smooth leather or kid suede. Inspect the closures. You want fine mixed hardware uniting cool silver and rose gold. Check the entire profile before you exit. Sling the strap over your shoulder and pivot in front of the glass. If this heavy object disrupts your balanced proportions, remove it immediately. Leave the bag behind if its buckles snag on an honest fabric like a lambswool cuff, or if the chain fights your neckline.
accessory_3.sectionIntro: Finish the look by verifying that every addition serves your structural foundation.

rankedItems.name: Small structured bag
rankedItems.useCase: kid suede or smooth leather top-handle, fine mixed hardware

tests: the take-off test

pattern_narrative.text: Anchor your print strategy in a cool temperature. Because your architecture requires balanced proportions, give tailored garments strict motifs like {recommended_pattern_1}. Let fluid pieces carry softer designs like {recommended_pattern_2}. As an adaptive dresser, shift the contrast scale to suit the environment. Build your looks by mixing a low-contrast tonal weave with a sharper graphic element as the context demands. Pass over anything flat or stiff. Leave {avoid_pattern_1} and {avoid_pattern_2} on the hanger entirely. Apply the stride analysis. Walk briskly across the floor and watch the garment move. The motif must read as a soft, pleasant texture in motion rather than a static block of ink. Rely on a visibly honest fabric to ground the design.
pattern_narrative.sectionIntro: Treat pattern as an extension of texture, using it to serve the physical architecture of your clothing.
tests: the passing test (reads as soft, pleasant texture in motion)

pattern_tip.text: Test a print by blurring your vision. Put the {recommended_pattern_1} piece on and step back from the glass. Do not focus on the individual shapes. Half-close your eyes and assess the entire surface. A successful design melts into a cohesive, fluid background rhythm. If the graphic leaps out sharply and disrupts your balanced proportions, leave the item on the hanger. You require a genuinely honest fabric that supports the structural cut rather than fighting it. Keep the ink cool and strict to ensure the motif serves your silhouette.
pattern_tip.sectionIntro: Apply the passing test to judge print scale and visual impact.
tests: the passing test (reads as soft, pleasant texture in motion)

venus_aries__moon_taurus__earth_dominant.coreFormula: clean impact + sensory comfort + one hot accent
venus_aries__moon_taurus__earth_dominant.closing: Your style guide is a map of your own instincts, built on clean impact + sensory comfort + one hot accent.

style_core.text:
Curate your pieces with slow, deliberate precision. Before you commit to a garment, test the physical weight of the fabric. Squeeze the wool or lift the silk. It must feel dense, substantial, and honest under your fingers. If it feels flimsy or hollow, leave it on the rail. Rely on the breath test as your final physical check. Put the piece on, drop your shoulders, and inhale deeply. The right garment settles against your skin and allows your chest to expand completely, grounding you without restricting your movement. Build your wardrobe slowly through these tactile rules.

Once your foundation is secure, deploy your outfits fast. Filter every combination through a strict equation: clean impact + sensory comfort + one hot accent. Picture a heavy camel cashmere sweater tucked into sharply pleated, warm taupe wool trousers. The precise cut provides the clean impact. The dense, soft yarn delivers the sensory comfort. Finish the look with a single point of heat. Add a heavy matt yellow gold cuff to your bare wrist or carry a burnt sienna leather pouch. Keep the baseline quiet and self-contained, letting that one concentrated accent anchor the look.
style_core.sectionIntro: This is your permanent style blueprint, a strict physical filter for testing every garment before you buy it.
tests: the breath test

textures_good.text: Sensory comfort is your primary filter. Your materials must deliver warm tactility without demanding your attention. Look for {texture_good_1} in your knitwear to provide layers that compress and bounce back. Anchor your footwear and belts with {texture_good_2}, a material that patinates and moulds to your shape over time. When you need clean impact, rely on {texture_good_3} for tailored coats that hold a sharp edge. Build your blouses and linings from {texture_good_4} for completely fluid movement. Trust your hands when shopping. Pinch the cloth firmly between your thumb and index finger. If the surface resists your skin or produces a faint synthetic drag, leave it on the rack. Here are your highest priority materials.
textures_good.sectionIntro: Filter your wardrobe through your hands first.

rankedItems.name: Cashmere
rankedItems.useCase: knitwear and winter layers that compress and bounce back

rankedItems.name: Full-grain leather
rankedItems.useCase: bags, shoes, and belts that mould to your life and patinate

rankedItems.name: Brushed wool and tweed
rankedItems.useCase: tailored coats and blazers that hold clean lines for years

rankedItems.name: Silk
rankedItems.useCase: blouses and linings that add fluid, effortless movement

rankedItems.name: Suede
rankedItems.useCase: shoes and accessories with quiet matt texture

rankedItems.name: Fine merino
rankedItems.useCase: base layers soft against the skin and resilient through washes

rankedItems.name: Linen
rankedItems.useCase: warmer-weather pieces whose natural wrinkle reads as authentic

tests: if a fabric resists your touch or feels squeaky between your fingers, leave it behind

textures_bad.text: Evaluate {texture_bad_1} by dropping it over your forearm. Reject anything rigid enough to stand away from your body. {texture_bad_2} will fight against your skin and ruin the drape required for a quiet base. You are building a contained, warm wardrobe. Roughness serves no purpose here. Scratch the surface of {texture_bad_3} with your fingernail. If it snags or bites back, it fails. A harsh material distracts the wearer. You require a flawless, yielding foundation before applying your one hot accent.
textures_bad.sectionIntro: Train your hands to reject textiles that fight against your body.
tests: if a fabric resists your touch or feels squeaky between your fingers, leave it behind

textures_sweet_spot.text: Achieve clean impact by mastering the pairing of {sweet_spot_keyword_1} and {sweet_spot_keyword_2}. Layer a dense, warm {texture_good_1} over a sleek knit. Test the resilience of the cloth by crushing a handful in your fist for ten seconds and releasing it. If the fibres hold harsh wrinkles or refuse to spring back, drop the garment. You require fibres that yield. Keep your choices physically distinct to maintain a self-contained profile. Let a heavy wrap rest directly on a smooth weave. Trust your own tension evaluation to confirm the quality.
textures_sweet_spot.sectionIntro: Combine your layers using tactile contrasts you can verify with a simple touch.
tests: if a fabric resists your touch or feels squeaky between your fingers, leave it behind

palette_narrative.text: Hold the cloth in direct sunlight and squint. If the dye appears chalky, leave it. Your spectrum runs unequivocally warm. Build your base using {core_colour_1} and {core_colour_2}. Bridge these with {core_colour_3} or {core_colour_4} for visual depth. Deploy {accent_colour_1} or {accent_colour_2} as a deliberate strike to ensure clean impact. Reach for your relief tone solely when the eye needs a pause from saturated heat. Walk straight past icy electric blue, neon brights, and fleeting trend pastels. Avoid the dissolution trap. This occurs when you layer pale gauze on pale gauze with nothing beneath, and the silhouette disappears. Fix this by restoring one line. Wear a bias-cut slip that traces the body, a fitted base layer, or a deep sea blue piece closest to the skin.
palette_narrative.sectionIntro: Mastering your palette means learning to distinguish shades that serve your structure from those that wash it out.

traps.failure: dissolution: pale gauze on pale gauze with nothing beneath and the silhouette disappears
traps.fix: restore one line: a bias-cut slip that traces the body, a fitted base layer, or the deep sea blue anchor worn closest to the skin

occasions_work.text: Let clean impact lead your choices for the office. You are building a strict, warm visual boundary around yourself. Handling a routine spreadsheet or disputing a supplier invoice does not require you to collapse your silhouette. Keep the tailoring sharp and the shoulders defined. A heavy camel wool blazer provides that necessary exterior shell. Underneath, a rich toffee cashmere polo shirt gives you a private tactile sanctuary. Finish with one hot accent to break the matt uniformity. Add a polished gold cuff or a bright silk pocket square. Stand in front of a mirror and reach for your collar. If the blazer loses its shape or the knitwear pulls tightly at the seams, take it off. Trust your physical feedback.
occasions_work.sectionIntro: This is how you structure your clothing to execute your work without losing your physical boundaries.
tests: paying a utility bill does not require collapsing your silhouette (Blaze)

occasions_intimate.text: In low light, fine details fade. Secure your baseline through strict, uncluttered shape. Reach for garments that feel substantial in the dark, like a dense brushed wool cardigan or a heavy silk shirt in oxblood. Test the textile by running the back of your hand along the grain. If the surface feels flimsy or catches on your skin, put it back. Sitting alone at your desk to pay a utility bill is no reason to let your silhouette collapse into a soft puddle of fleece. Maintain a distinct shoulder line. Keep the overall temperature warm with structured warm taupe trousers. Deliver one hot accent by placing a matt gold ring next to a polished brass watch.
occasions_intimate.sectionIntro: Maintain your standards in private spaces by letting strict silhouettes do the work of daylight.
tests: paying a utility bill does not require collapsing your silhouette (Blaze)

occasions_daily.text: Every ordinary errand sets your baseline. Picking up a prescription does not justify dissolving into shapeless loungewear. Maintain your lines even on a quiet Tuesday morning. Reach for a rich toffee brushed wool coat over simple silk separates. Weigh the outerwear by lifting it from the collar with two fingers. If it lacks the mass to pull down firmly against gravity, leave the piece on the hanger. The standard remains absolute across all environments. Adhere to your guiding logic without hesitation. Your daily routines rely entirely on clean impact + sensory comfort + one hot accent. Finish the look with a warm brass cuff or a sharp full-grain leather boot.
occasions_daily.sectionIntro: Treat your mundane schedule as the primary proving ground for your wardrobe.
tests: paying a utility bill does not require collapsing your silhouette (Blaze)

hardware_metals.text: Your metal strategy relies on a warm foundation. Anchor your wardrobe with {metal_1} and {metal_2}. Introduce {metal_3} only when a specific item demands it. The eye is easily tricked by hollow alloys. Test every chain or buckle by dropping it onto a wooden counter. Appearance deceives, but a dense, resonant clack tells the truth. Trust the acoustic weight. Avoid blindingly reflective cool hardware. A glaring silver zip cuts harshly across subdued warm layers. Rely on matt or brushed surfaces rather than {excluded_finish} to keep the visual whole unified. This physical grounding secures the clean impact you need.
hardware_metals.sectionIntro: Judge your hardware by its weight, not its reflection.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

hardware_stones.text: Examine the piece closely before purchasing. Your eyes can be fooled by clever cutting, but temperature is absolute. Press a genuine {stone_1} against your wrist. It will remain distinctly cold against your skin before slowly warming up. This undeniable physical reality becomes your baseline. Use that dense mineral to provide your one hot accent. Avoid mirror-polished settings. A highly reflective surface creates a sharp, glaring distraction against warm, subdued layers. Choose a matt setting for {stone_2} or {stone_3}. This subdues the glare and ensures the jewellery remains strictly self-contained.
hardware_stones.sectionIntro: Select minerals that ground your look with unmistakable physical density.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

hardware_tip.text: Pick up every {metal_1} ring or {stone_1} pendant. Visuals lie. Mass tells the truth. Balance the item on the tip of your index finger to confirm its substance. If it feels hollow or feather-light, leave it on the shelf. Next, evaluate the surface finish. A high-gloss polish creates a sharp visual fracture against your subdued fabrics. Stick to a matt texture to keep your overall aesthetic cohesive. This deliberate choice ensures your jewellery provides true sensory comfort, rather than a jarring disruption.
hardware_tip.sectionIntro: Test every piece of hardware for true substance and cohesion before you wear it.
tests: pick it up: looks lie, weight doesn't (the pick-it-up weight test)

traps.failure: high-shine, polished chrome silver looks sharp and distracting next to soft muted colours
traps.fix: stick to matt and brushed textures to keep the look cohesive

accessory_1.text: You have secured your base with clean impact and sensory comfort. Now you must deliver the one hot accent. Enforce a strict limit of one or two strong pieces per look. Start with handbags. Seek out full-grain leather with a matt body, anchored by exactly one polished clasp in a warm metal. Test the density of the hide before you buy. Set the empty bag upright on a flat surface and step back. It must hold its architectural shape entirely on its own. If the leather slumps or collapses inward, the material is too thin. Reject it.
accessory_1.sectionIntro: Accessories are the final point of focus, directing the eye exactly where you want it.

rankedItems.name: Handbags
rankedItems.useCase: full-grain leather, matt body with one polished clasp

tests: the float test

accessory_2.text: Scarves demand careful material selection. Stick to silk in a strictly matt weave. Give every piece the drop test before buying. Lift a corner of the scarf high above a counter and release it. Watch how it falls. A worthy matt silk settles immediately into a dense puddle. If the fabric catches the air and flutters wildly, or holds a rigid crease, put it back. That heavy drape provides a warm layer at the collar. Rely on a dyed silk in deep saffron or oxblood to deliver one hot accent against a classic camel sweater. Do not compromise on the density of the weave.
accessory_2.sectionIntro: Test your neckwear with your own hands to guarantee material quality.

rankedItems.name: Scarves
rankedItems.useCase: silk, matt

tests: the float test

accessory_3.text: Choose keepsake jewellery that combines mixed patina and polish, favouring aged brass with a polished gold addition. Secure your garments using belts cut from full-grain leather and fastened by a brushed buckle. Apply the tension test to these accessories. Bend the leather strap sharply back on itself. If the surface cracks or feels papery, take it off immediately. You require items that possess intrinsic structural integrity. Only dense, warm metals and thick, supple belts provide the grounding needed to land your one hot accent.
accessory_3.sectionIntro: Anchor your finishing items by testing their physical gravity.

rankedItems.name: Keepsake jewellery
rankedItems.useCase: aged brass with a polished gold accent, mixed patina and polish

rankedItems.name: Belts
rankedItems.useCase: full-grain leather, brushed buckle

tests: the float test

pattern_narrative.text: Keep pattern contrast low and strictly tonal. Tailored trousers and structured coats must draw their pattern from the physical weave of the cloth. Fluid silk shirts may carry a print, but only if they pass the underwater test. Hold the garment under a warm lamp and gently swing the fabric. The design must ripple and blend like shifting sunlight on a shallow seabed. If the lines read like sharp graphic signage, drop the piece. Build your foundation with {recommended_pattern_1} and {recommended_pattern_2}. Pass over {avoid_pattern_1} and {avoid_pattern_2} entirely. A subtle woven pattern guarantees clean impact, while a quiet printed silk delivers sensory comfort. Create your one hot accent with a single piece of jewellery instead of relying on high-contrast garments.
pattern_narrative.sectionIntro: Treat pattern as a strict textural exercise, never a focal point.
tests: the underwater test (read like light moving on the seabed, not signage)

pattern_tip.text: Apply the distance blur test to every patterned garment. Drape a piece of {recommended_pattern_1} over a chair across the room and narrow your eyes. The shapes must dissolve into shifting, warm shadows. Think of a landscape viewed through frosted glass. If the edges remain stark and readable like a barcode, reject the piece immediately. Rigid geometry fights your natural grounding. You require borders that bleed gently into one another. This liquid transition provides visual calm and guarantees clean impact.
pattern_tip.sectionIntro: Use the underwater test to evaluate the starkness and legibility of any print.
tests: the underwater test (read like light moving on the seabed, not signage)
